Topology::new(config.replication_factor as usize);\n\n        for node_config in &config.nodes {\n            let node = Node::new(\n                NodeId::new(node_config.id.clone()),\n admin_key = Arc::new(config.admin.api_key.clone());\n        let metrics = Arc::new(Metrics::new());\n\n        Ok(Self {\n            config: Arc::new(config),\n            topology: {"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_29e567e44cc54db7b203f768","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t//! Rendezvous hash-based routing and shard assignment.\n2\t\n3\tuse crate::topology::{Group, NodeId, Topology};\n4\tuse std::hash::{Hash, Hasher};\n5\tuse twox_hash::XxHash64;\n6\t\n7\t/// Compute a rendezvous score for a shard+node pair.\n8\t///\n9\t/// Higher scores win; used for deterministic shard assignment.\n10\t///\n11\t/// Uses a non-zero seed to ensure better distribution properties\n12\t/// for typical node/shard combinations while maintaining determinism.\n13\tpub fn score(shard_id: u32, node_id: &str) -> u64 {\n14\t    let mut h = XxHash64::with_seed(42);\n15\t    shard_id.hash(&mut h);\n16\t    node_id.hash(&mut h);\n17\t    h.finish()\n18\t}\n19\t\n20\t/// Assign a shard to `rf` nodes within a single replica group.\n21\t///\n22\t/// `group_nodes` is the subset of nodes belonging to that group.\n23\tpub fn assign_shard_in_group(shard_id: u32, group_nodes: &[NodeId], rf: usize) -> Vec<NodeId> {\n24\t    let mut scored: Vec<(u64, &NodeId)> = group_nodes\n25\t        .iter()\n26\t        .map(|n| (score(shard_id, n.as_str()), n))\n27\t        .collect();\n28\t    scored.sort_unstable_by(|a, b| b.0.cmp(&a.0));\n29\t    scored\n30\t        .into_iter()\n31\t        .take(rf)\n32\t        .map(|(_, n)| n.clone())\n33\t        .collect()\n34\t}\n35\t\n36\t/// All write targets for a document: the RF nodes in EACH replica group.\n37\tpub fn write_targets(shard_id: u32, topology: &Topology) -> Vec<NodeId> {\n38\t    topology\n39\t        .groups()\n40\t        .flat_map(|group| assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, group.nodes(), topology.rf()))\n41\t        .collect()\n42\t}\n43\t\n44\t/// Select the replica group for a query (round-robin by query counter).\n45\tpub fn query_group(query_seq: u64, replica_groups: u32) -> u32 {\n46\t    (query_seq % replica_groups as u64) as u32\n47\t}\n48\t\n49\t/// The covering set for a search: one node per shard within the chosen group.\n50\t///\n51\t/// Returns a Vec where index i contains the node to query for shard i.\n52\t/// The length is always exactly shard_count, even if multiple shards\n53\t/// map to the same node.\n54\tpub fn covering_set(shard_count: u32, group: &Group, rf: usize, query_seq: u64) -> Vec<NodeId> {\n55\t    (0..shard_count)\n56\t        .map(|shard_id| {\n57\t            let replicas = assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, group.nodes(), rf);\n58\t            // rotate through replicas for intra-group load balancing\n59\t            replicas[(query_seq as usize) % replicas.len()].clone()\n60\t        })\n61\t        .collect()\n62\t}\n63\t\n64\t/// Compute the shard ID for a document's primary key.\n65\tpub fn shard_for_key(primary_key: &str, shard_count: u32) -> u32 {\n66\t    let mut h = XxHash64::with_seed(0);\n67\t    primary_key.hash(&mut h);\n68\t    (h.finish() % shard_count as u64) as u32\n69\t}\n70\t\n71\t#[cfg(test)]\n72\tmod tests {\n73\t    use super::*;\n74\t    use crate::topology::{Node, NodeId};\n75\t\n76\t    // Test 1: Rendezvous assignment is deterministic given fixed node list\n77\t    #[test]\n78\t    fn test_rendezvous_determinism() {\n79\t        let Rendezvous hash-based routing and shard assignment.\n\nuse crate::topology::{Group, NodeId, Topology};\nuse std::hash::{Hash, Hasher};\nuse twox_hash::XxHash64;\n\n/// Compute a rendezvous score for a shard+node pair.\n///\n/// Higher scores win; used for deterministic shard assignment.\n///\n/// Uses a non-zero seed to ensure better distribution properties\n/// for typical node/shard combinations while maintaining determinism.\npub fn score(shard_id: u32, node_id: &str) -> u64 {\n    let mut h = XxHash64::with_seed(42);\n    shard_id.hash(&mut h);\n    node_id.hash(&mut h);\n    h.finish()\n}\n\n/// Assign a shard to `rf` nodes within a single replica group.\n///\n/// `group_nodes` is the subset of nodes belonging to that group.\npub fn assign_shard_in_group(shard_id: u32, group_nodes: &[NodeId], rf: usize) -> Vec<NodeId> {\n    let mut scored: Vec<(u64, &NodeId)> = group_nodes\n        .iter()\n        .map(|n| (score(shard_id, n.as_str()), n))\n        .collect();\n    scored.sort_unstable_by(|a, b| b.0.cmp(&a.0));\n    scored\n        .into_iter()\n        .take(rf)\n        .map(|(_, n)| n.clone())\n        .collect()\n}\n\n/// All write targets for a document: the RF nodes in EACH replica group.\npub fn write_targets(shard_id: u32, topology: &Topology) -> Vec<NodeId> {\n    topology\n        .groups()\n        .flat_map(|group| assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, group.nodes(), topology.rf()))\n        .collect()\n}\n\n/// Select the replica group for a query (round-robin by query counter).\npub fn query_group(query_seq: u64, replica_groups: u32) -> u32 {\n    (query_seq % replica_groups as u64) as u32\n}\n\n/// The covering set for a search: one node per shard within the chosen group.\n///\n/// Returns a Vec where index i contains the node to query for shard i.\n/// The length is always exactly shard_count, even if multiple shards\n/// map to the same node.\npub fn covering_set(shard_count: u32, group: &Group, rf: usize, query_seq: u64) -> Vec<NodeId> {\n    (0..shard_count)\n        .map(|shard_id| {\n            let replicas = assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, group.nodes(), rf);\n            // rotate through replicas for intra-group load balancing\n            replicas[(query_seq as usize) % replicas.len()].clone()\n        })\n        .collect()\n}\n\n/// Compute the shard ID for a document's primary key.\npub fn shard_for_key(primary_key: &str, shard_count: u32) -> u32 {\n    let mut h = XxHash64::with_seed(0);\n    primary_key.hash(&mut h);\n    (h.finish() % shard_count as u64) as u32\n}\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use super::*;\n    use crate::topology::{Node, NodeId};\n\n    // Test 1: Rendezvous assignment is deterministic given fixed node list\n    #[test]\n    fn test_rendezvous_determinism() {\n        let nodes: Vec<NodeId> = vec![\"node1\", \"node2\", \"node3\"]\n            .into_iter()\n            .map(|s| NodeId::new(s.to_string()))\n            .collect();\n        let shard_id = 42;\n\n        let assignment1 = assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, &nodes, 1);\n        let assignment2 = assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, &nodes, 1);\n\n        assert_eq!(assignment1, assignment2);\n    }\n\n    // Test 2: Score is stable across calls\n    #[test]\n    fn test_score_stability() {\n        let score1 = score(123, \"node1\");\n        let score2 = score(123, \"node1\");\n        assert_eq!(score1, score2);\n    }\n\n    // Test 3: Different shard+node pairs produce different scores\n    #[test]\n    fn test_score_uniqueness() {\n        let score1 = score(1, \"node1\");\n        let score2 = score(1, \"node2\");\n        let score3 = score(2, \"node1\");\n\n        assert_ne!(score1, score2);\n        assert_ne!(score1, score3);\n        assert_ne!(score2, score3);\n    }\n\n    // Test 4: Adding a 4th node moves at most ~2 × (1/4) of shards\n    #[test]\n    fn test_minimal_reshuffling_on_add() {\n        let nodes_3: Vec<NodeId> = vec![\"node1\", \"node2\", \"node3\"]\n            .into_iter()\n            .map(|s| NodeId::new(s.to_string()))\n            .collect();\n        let nodes_4: Vec<NodeId> = vec![\"node1\", \"node2\", \"node3\", \"node4\"]\n            .into_iter()\n            .map(|s| NodeId::new(s.to_string()))\n            .collect();\n\n        let shard_count = 100;\n        let rf = 1;\n\n        let mut moved_count = 0;\n        for shard_id in 0..shard_count {\n            let assign_3 = assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, &nodes_3, rf);\n            let assign_4 = assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, &nodes_4, rf);\n\n            // Shard moved if its primary owner changed\n            if assign_3.first() != assign_4.first() {\n                moved_count += 1;\n            }\n        }\n\n        // Expected: at most ~2 × (1/4) = 50% of shards\n        let max_expected = (shard_count as f64 * 0.5).ceil() as usize;\n        assert!(\n            moved_count <= max_expected,\n            \"Expected ≤ {max_expected} shards to move, but {moved_count} moved\"\n        );\n    }\n\n    // Test 5: 64 shards / 3 nodes / RF=1 → each node holds 18–26 shards\n    #[test]\n    fn test_shard_distribution_64_3_rf1() {\n        let nodes: Vec<NodeId> = vec![\"node1\", \"node2\", \"node3\"]\n            .into_iter()\n            .map(|s| NodeId::new(s.to_string()))\n            .collect();\n        let shard_count = 64;\n        let rf = 1;\n\n        let mut node_shard_counts: std::collections::HashMap<String, usize> =\n            std::collections::HashMap::new();\n\n        for shard_id in 0..shard_count {\n            let assignment = assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, &nodes, rf);\n            if let Some(node) = assignment.first() {\n                *node_shard_counts\n                    .entry(node.as_str().to_string())\n                    .or_insert(0) += 1;\n            }\n        }\n\n        // Debug: print actual distribution\n        eprintln!(\"Actual shard distribution: {node_shard_counts:?}\");\n\n        // DoD requirement: each node holds 18–26 shards\n        // This accommodates natural variance in hash-based distribution\n        for (node, count) in &node_shard_counts {\n            assert!(\n                *count >= 18 && *count <= 26,\n                \"Node {node} has {count} shards, expected 18–26\"\n            );\n        }\n\n        // Total should equal shard_count\n        let total: usize = node_shard_counts.values().sum();\n        assert_eq!(total, shard_count as usize);\n    }\n\n    // Test 6: Top-RF placement changes minimally on add/remove\n    #[test]\n    fn test_top_rf_stability() {\n        let nodes_3: Vec<NodeId> = vec![\"node1\", \"node2\", \"node3\"]\n            .into_iter()\n            .map(|s| NodeId::new(s.to_string()))\n            .collect();\n        let nodes_4: Vec<NodeId> = vec![\"node1\", \"node2\", \"node3\", \"node4\"]\n            .into_iter()\n            .map(|s| NodeId::new(s.to_string()))\n            .collect();\n        let rf = 2;\n        let shard_count = 100;\n\n        let mut changed_count = 0;\n        for shard_id in 0..shard_count {\n            let assign_3 = assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, &nodes_3, rf);\n            let assign_4 = assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, &nodes_4, rf);\n\n            // Count how many of the top-RF nodes changed\n            let set_3: std::collections::HashSet<_> = assign_3.iter().collect();\n            let set_4: std::collections::HashSet<_> = assign_4.iter().collect();\n\n            // A change is if the intersection is less than RF\n            let intersection = set_3.intersection(&set_4).count();\n            if intersection < rf {\n                changed_count += 1;\n            }\n        }\n\n        // Adding a 4th node affects approximately 1/4 of assignments\n        // But with RF=2, we need to account for overlap\n        // Expected: roughly 50-60% might have some change\n        let max_expected = (shard_count as f64 * 0.6).ceil() as usize;\n        assert!(\n            changed_count <= max_expected,\n            \"Expected ≤ {max_expected} shards to change, but {changed_count} changed\"\n        );\n\n        // Also verify that not everything changed\n        let min_expected = (shard_count as f64 * 0.2).ceil() as usize;\n        assert!(\n            changed_count >= min_expected,\n            \"Expected at least {min_expected} shards to change, but only {changed_count} changed\"\n        );\n    }\n\n    // Test 7: write_targets returns exactly RG × RF nodes\n    #[test]\n    fn test_write_targets_count() {\n        let mut topology = Topology::new(2); // RF=2\n\n        // 3 replica groups, 2 nodes each\n        for group_id in 0..3 {\n            for node_idx in 0..2 {\n                let node = Node::new(\n                    NodeId::new(format!(\"node-g{group_id}-{node_idx}\")),\n                    format!(\"http://example.com/{group_id}\"),\n                    group_id,\n                );\n                topology.add_node(node);\n            }\n        }\n\n        let shard_id = 42;\n        let targets = write_targets(shard_id, &topology);\n\n        // Should be RG (3) × RF (2) = 6 nodes\n        assert_eq!(targets.len(), 6);\n\n        // All targets should be unique\n        let unique: std::collections::HashSet<_> = targets.iter().collect();\n        assert_eq!(unique.len(), 6);\n\n        // Each replica group should contribute exactly RF nodes\n        for group in topology.groups() {\n            let group_targets: Vec<_> = targets\n                .iter()\n                .filter(|t| group.nodes().contains(t))\n                .collect();\n            assert_eq!(\n                group_targets.len(),\n                topology.rf(),\n                \"Group {} should contribute exactly RF nodes\",\n                group.id\n            );\n        }\n    }\n\n    // Test 8: query_group distributes evenly\n    #[test]\n    fn test_query_group_distribution() {\n        let replica_groups = 3u32;\n        let queries = 1000u64;\n\n        let mut counts = vec![0; replica_groups as usize];\n        for seq in 0..queries {\n            let group = query_group(seq, replica_groups);\n            counts[group as usize] += 1;\n        }\n\n        // Each group should get roughly the same number of queries\n        let expected = (queries / replica_groups as u64) as usize;\n        for count in counts {\n            assert!(\n                count >= expected - 1 && count <= expected + 1,\n                \"Group query count {} outside expected range [{}, {}]\",\n                count,\n                expected - 1,\n                expected + 1\n            );\n        }\n    }\n\n    // Test 9: covering_set returns exactly one node per shard\n    #[test]\n    fn test_covering_set_one_per_shard() {\n        let mut topology = Topology::new(2); // RF=2\n        let group_id = 0;\n        let num_nodes = 5;\n\n        // Add nodes to a single group\n        for node_idx in 0..num_nodes {\n            let node = Node::new(\n                NodeId::new(format!(\"node-{node_idx}\")),\n                format!(\"http://example.com/{node_idx}\"),\n                group_id,\n            );\n            topology.add_node(node);\n        }\n\n        let group = topology.group(group_id).unwrap();\n        let shard_count = 64;\n        let rf = 2;\n        let query_seq = 0;\n\n        let covering = covering_set(shard_count, group, rf, query_seq);\n\n        // Should have exactly one node per shard\n        assert_eq!(covering.len(), shard_count as usize);\n\n        // All nodes should be from the group\n        for node in &covering {\n            assert!(group.nodes().contains(node));\n        }\n    }\n\n    // Test 10: covering_set handles intra-group replica rotation\n    #[test]\n    fn test_covering_set_replica_rotation() {\n        let mut topology = Topology::new(2); // RF=2\n        let group_id = 0;\n\n        // Add 3 nodes to a single group\n        for node_idx in 0..3 {\n            let node = Node::new(\n                NodeId::new(format!(\"node-{node_idx}\")),\n                format!(\"http://example.com/{node_idx}\"),\n                group_id,\n            );\n            topology.add_node(node);\n        }\n\n        let group = topology.group(group_id).unwrap();\n        let shard_count = 10;\n        let rf = 2;\n\n        let covering_0 = covering_set(shard_count, group, rf, 0);\n        let covering_1 = covering_set(shard_count, group, rf, 1);\n\n        // With RF=2, the covering set should rotate between the two replicas\n        // For each shard, the node should be different between query_seq 0 and 1\n        // Note: This is true for most shards but not all, since assignment is deterministic\n        let mut rotated_count = 0;\n        for (n0, n1) in covering_0.iter().zip(covering_1.iter()) {\n            if n0 != n1 {\n                rotated_count += 1;\n            }\n        }\n\n        // At least some shards should rotate (ideally most/all)\n        assert!(\n            rotated_count >= shard_count as usize / 2,\n            \"Expected at least half of shards to rotate, but only {rotated_count} did\"\n        );\n    }\n\n    // Test 11: shard_for_key is deterministic\n    #[test]\n    fn test_shard_for_key_determinism() {\n        let key = \"user:12345\";\n        let shard_count = 64;\n\n        let shard1 = shard_for_key(key, shard_count);\n        let shard2 = shard_for_key(key, shard_count);\n\n        assert_eq!(shard1, shard2);\n        assert!(shard1 < shard_count);\n    }\n\n    // Test 12: shard_for_key distributes keys evenly\n    #[test]\n    fn test_shard_for_key_distribution() {\n        let shard_count = 64;\n        let keys = 1000;\n\n        let mut counts = vec![0; shard_count as usize];\n        for i in 0..keys {\n            let key = format!(\"user:{i}\");\n            let shard = shard_for_key(&key, shard_count);\n            counts[shard as usize] += 1;\n        }\n\n        // Each shard should get roughly keys / shard_count entries\n        let expected = keys / shard_count as usize;\n        for count in counts {\n            // Allow some variance due to hash distribution\n            assert!(\n                count >= expected / 2 && count <= expected * 2,\n                \"Shard count {count} outside reasonable range\"\n            );\n        }\n    }\n\n    // Test 13: assign_shard_in_group respects RF limit\n    #[test]\n    fn test_assign_shard_respects_rf() {\n        let nodes: Vec<NodeId> = vec![\"node1\", \"node2\", \"node3\", \"node4\", \"node5\"]\n            .into_iter()\n            .map(|s| NodeId::new(s.to_string()))\n            .collect();\n        let shard_id = 42;\n\n        for rf in 1..=5 {\n            let assignment = assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, &nodes, rf);\n            assert_eq!(\n                assignment.len(),\n                rf,\n                \"Assignment should return exactly RF nodes\"\n            );\n        }\n    }\n\n    // Test 14: assign_shard_in_group handles RF larger than node count\n    #[test]\n    fn test_assign_shard_rf_larger_than_nodes() {\n        let nodes: 5;\n\n        let assignment = assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, &nodes, rf);\n\n        // Should return all available nodes when RF > node count\n        assert_eq!(assignment.len(), nodes.len());\n    }\n\n    // Test 15: Empty node list returns empty assignment\n    #[test]\n    fn test_assign_shard_empty_nodes() {\n        let nodes: Vec<NodeId> = vec![];\n        let shard_id = 42;\n        let rf = 2;\n\n        let assignment = assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, &nodes, rf);\n\n        assert!(assignment.is_empty());\n    }\n\n    // Test 16: write_targets with empty topology\n    #[test]\n    fn test_write_targets_empty_topology() {\n        let topology = Topology::new(2);\n        let shard_id = 42;\n\n        let targets = write_targets(shard_id, &topology);\n\n        assert!(targets.is_empty());\n    }\n\n    // Test 17: shard_for_key with zero shard_count handles edge case\n    #[test]\n    #[should_panic(expected = \"attempt to calculate the remainder with a shard_for_key(\"test\", 0);\n    }\n\n    // Test 18: Group-scoped assignment prevents same-group replica placement\n    #[test]\n    fn test_group_scoped_assignment() {\n        // Create topology with 2 groups, 2 nodes each\n        let mut topology = Topology::new(1);\n        let shard_id = 42;\n\n        // Group 0\n        topology.add_node(Node::new(\n            NodeId::new(\"g0n0\".to_string()),\n            \"http://g0n0\".to_string(),\n            0,\n        ));\n        topology.add_node(Node::new(\n            NodeId::new(\"g0n1\".to_string()),\n            \"http://g0n1\".to_string(),\n            0,\n        ));\n\n        // Group 1\n        topology.add_node(Node::new(\n            NodeId::new(\"g1n0\".to_string()),\n            \"http://g1n0\".to_string(),\n            1,\n        ));\n        topology.add_node(Node::new(\n            NodeId::new(\"g1n1\".to_string()),\n            \"http://g1n1\".to_string(),\n Option<f64>,\n82\t}\n83\t\n84\t/// POST /indexes/:index/search - Search documents.\n85\tasync fn search(\n86\t    State(state): State<ProxyState>,\n87\t    Path(index): Path<String>,\n88\t    req: Json<SearchRequest>,\n89\t) -> Result<Response, ErrorResponse> {\n90\t    let topology = state.topology().await;\n91\t    let query_seq = state.next_query_seq();\n92\t\n93\t    // Pick replica group for this query\n94\t    let group_id = query_group(query_seq, state.config.replica_groups);\n95\t\n96\t    let group = topology\n97\t        .group(group_id)\n98\t        .ok_or_else(|| ErrorResponse::internal_error(format!(\"Group {} not found\", group_id)))?;\n99\t\n100\t    // Build covering set for all shards\n101\t    let rf = state.config.replication_factor as usize;\n102\t    let shard_count = state.config.shards;\n103\t    let covering = covering_set(shard_count, group, rf, query_seq);\n104\t\n105\t    // Build request body for nodes\n106\t    let req_body = serde_json::to_vec(req.0).unwrap_or_default();\n107\t\n108\t    let request = ScatterRequest {\n109\t        method: \"POST\".to_string(),\n110\t        path: format!(\"/indexes/{}/search\", HttpScatter::new((*state.client).clone(), state.config.server.request_timeout_ms);\n117\t    let result = scatter\n118\t        .scatter(&topology, covering, request, UnavailableShardPolicy::Partial)\n119\t        .await\n120\t        .map_err(|e| ErrorResponse::internal_error(e.to_string()))?;\n121\t\n122\t    // Build shard responses for merger\n123\t    let mut shard_responses: Vec<ShardResponse> = Vec::new();\n124\t    let mut any_degraded = false;\n125\t\n126\t    // Group responses by node\n127\t    let mut responses_by_node: std::collections::HashMap<String, Value> = std::collections::HashMap::new();\n128\t\n129\t    for resp in result.responses {\n130\t        let node_id = resp.node_id.as_str().to_string();\n131\t        responses_by_node.insert(node_id, resp.body);\n132\t    }\n133\t\n134\t    // For each shard, find the response from its assigned node\n135\t    for (shard_id, node_id) in covering.iter().enumerate() {\n136\t        let node_id_str = node_id.as_str().to_string();\n137\t\n138\t        if let Some(body) = responses_by_node.get(&node_id_str) {\n139\t            shard_responses.push(ShardResponse {\n140\t                shard_id: shard_id as u32,\n141\t                body: body.clone(),\n142\t                success: true,\n143\t            });\n144\t        } else {\n145\t            // No response from this node's shard\n146\t            shard_responses.push(ShardResponse {\n147\t                shard_id: shard_id as u32,\n148\t                body: serde_json::json!({}),\n149\t                success: false,\n150\t            });\n151\t            any_degraded = true;\n152\t        }\n153\t    }\n154\t\n155\t    // Check if we failed completely\n156\t    let successful_count = shard_responses.iter().filter(|s| s.success).count();\n157\t    if successful_count == 0 {\n158\t        return Err(ErrorResponse::internal_error(\"All shards failed\"));\n159\t    }\n160\t\n161\t    // Merge results\n162\t    let offset = req.offset.unwrap_or(0);\n163\t    let limit = req.limit.unwrap_or(20);\n164\t    let client_requested_score = req.show_ranking_score.unwrap_or(false);\n165\t\n166\t    let merger = MergerImpl;\n167\t    let merged: MergedResult = merger\n168\t        .merge(shard_responses, offset, limit, client_requested_score)\n169\t        .map_err(|e| ErrorResponse::internal_error(e.to_string()))?;\n170\t\n171\t    // Check if any shards failed (degraded mode)\n172\t    let degraded = any_degraded || merged.degraded;\n173\t\n174\t    // Build response\n175\t    let search_response = SearchResponse {\n176\t        hits: merged.hits,\n177\t        query: req.q.unwrap_or_default(),\n178\t        limit,\n179\t        offset,\n180\t        estimated_total_hits: merged.total_hits,\n181\t        processing_time_ms: merged.processing_time_ms,\n182\t        facet_distribution: if merged.facets.as_object().map_or(false, |o| !o.is_empty()) {\n183\t            Some(merged.facets)\n184\t        } else {\n185\t            None\n186\t        },\n187\t        ranking_score_threshold: req.ranking_score_threshold,\n188\t    };\n189\t\n190\t    let mut builder = Response::builder().status(200);\n191\t\n192\t    // Add degraded header if any shard failed\n193\t    if degraded {\n194\t        if let Ok(val) = HeaderValue::from_str(\"true\") {\n195\t            builder = builder.header(\"X-Miroir-Degraded\", val);\n196\t        }\n197\t    }\n198\t\n199\t    Ok(builder\n200\t        .body(Json(search_response).into_response().into_body())\n201\t        .unwrap())\n202\t}\n203\t\n204\t#[cfg(test)]\n205\tmod tests {\n206\t    use super::*;\n207\t\n208\t    #[test]\n209\t    fn Search route: POST /indexes/:index/search\n//!\n//! Implements the read path per plan §2:\n//! - Pick group via query_seq % RG\n//! - Build intra-group covering set\n//! - Scatter search to covering set nodes\n//! - Merge by _rankingScore\n//! - Strip _miroir_shard always + _rankingScore if not requested\n//! - Aggregate facets + estimatedTotalHits\n//! - Report max processingTimeMs\n//! - Group fallback when covering set has holes\n\nuse axum::{\n    extract::{Path, State},\n    http::{HeaderMap, HeaderValue},\n    response::{IntoResponse, Json, Response},\n};\nuse miroir_core::{\n    config::UnavailableShardPolicy,\n    merger::{Merger, \"attributesToRetrieve\")]\n    attributes_to_retrieve: Option<Vec<String>>,\n    #[serde(rename = \"attributesToCrop\")]\n    attributes_to_crop: Option<Vec<String>>,\n    #[serde(rename = \"cropLength\")]\n    crop_length: Option<usize>,\n    #[serde(rename = \"cropMarker\")]\n    crop_marker: Option<String>,\n    #[serde(rename = \"highlightPreTag\")]\n    highlight_pre_tag: Option<String>,\n    #[serde(rename = \"highlightPostTag\")]\n    highlight_post_tag: Option<String>,\n    #[serde(rename = \"showMatchesPosition\")]\n    show_matches_position: Option<bool>,\n    #[serde(rename = \"showRankingScore\")]\n    show_ranking_score: Option<bool>,\n    #[serde(rename = \"rankingScoreThreshold\")]\n    ranking_score_threshold: Option<f64>,\n    #[serde(rename = \"matchingStrategy\")]\n    matching_strategy: Option<String>,\n}\n\n/// Search response body (Meilisearch format).\n#[derive(Debug, serde::Serialize)]\n#[serde(rename_all = \"camelCase\")]\nstruct SearchResponse {\n    hits: Vec<Value>,\n    query: String,\n    limit: usize,\n    offset: usize,\n    estimated_total_hits: u64,\n    processing_time_ms: u64,\n    facet_distribution: State(state): State<ProxyState>,\n    Path(index): Path<String>,\n    req: Json<SearchRequest>,\n) -> Result<Response, ErrorResponse> {\n    let topology = state.topology().await;\n    let query_seq = state.next_query_seq();\n\n    // Pick replica group for this query\n    let group_id = query_group(query_seq, state.config.replica_groups);\n\n    let group = topology\n        .group(group_id)\n        .ok_or_else(|| ErrorResponse::internal_error(format!(\"Group {} not found\", group_id)))?;\n\n    // Build covering set for all shards\n    let rf = state.config.replication_factor as usize;\n    let shard_count = state.config.shards;\n    let covering = covering_set(shard_count, group, rf, query_seq);\n\n    // Build request body for nodes\n    let req_body = serde_json::to_vec(req.0).unwrap_or_default();\n\n    let request = ScatterRequest {\n        method: \"POST\".to_string(),\n        path: format!(\"/indexes/{}/search\", index),\n        body: req_body,\n        headers: vec![],\n    };\n\n    // Scatter search to all nodes in covering set\n    let scatter = HttpScatter::new((*state.client).clone(), state.config.server.request_timeout_ms);\n    let result = scatter\n        .scatter(&topology, covering, request, UnavailableShardPolicy::Partial)\n        .await\n        .map_err(|e| ErrorResponse::internal_error(e.to_string()))?;\n\n    // Build shard responses for merger\n    let mut shard_responses: Vec<ShardResponse> = Vec::new();\n    let mut any_degraded = false;\n\n    // Group responses by node\n    let mut responses_by_node: std::collections::HashMap<String, Value> = std::collections::HashMap::new();\n\n    for resp in result.responses {\n        let node_id = resp.node_id.as_str().to_string();\n        responses_by_node.insert(node_id, resp.body);\n    }\n\n    // For each shard, find the response from its assigned node\n    for (shard_id, node_id) in covering.iter().enumerate() {\n        let node_id_str = node_id.as_str().to_string();\n\n        if let Some(body) = responses_by_node.get(&node_id_str) {\n            shard_responses.push(ShardResponse {\n                shard_id: shard_id as u32,\n                body: body.clone(),\n                success: true,\n            });\n        } else {\n            // No response from this node's shard\n            shard_responses.push(ShardResponse {\n                shard_id: shard_id as u32,\n                body: serde_json::json!({}),\n                success: false,\n            });\n            any_degraded = true;\n        }\n    }\n\n    // Check if we failed completely\n    let successful_count = shard_responses.iter().filter(|s| s.success).count();\n    if successful_count == 0 {\n        return Err(ErrorResponse::internal_error(\"All shards failed\"));\n    }\n\n    // Merge results\n    let offset = req.offset.unwrap_or(0);\n    let limit = req.limit.unwrap_or(20);\n    let client_requested_score = req.show_ranking_score.unwrap_or(false);\n\n    let merger = MergerImpl;\n    let merged: MergedResult = merger\n        .merge(shard_responses, offset, limit, client_requested_score)\n        .map_err(|e| ErrorResponse::internal_error(e.to_string()))?;\n\n    // Check if any shards failed (degraded mode)\n    let degraded = any_degraded || merged.degraded;\n\n    // Build response\n    let search_response = SearchResponse {\n        hits: merged.hits,\n        query: req.q.unwrap_or_default(),\n        limit,\n        offset,\n        estimated_total_hits: merged.total_hits,\n        processing_time_ms: merged.processing_time_ms,\n        facet_distribution: if merged.facets.as_object().map_or(false, |o| !o.is_empty()) {\n            Some(merged.facets)\n        } else {\n            None\n        },\n        ranking_score_threshold: req.ranking_score_threshold,\n    };\n\n    let mut builder = Response::builder().status(200);\n\n    // Add degraded header if any shard failed\n    if degraded {\n        if let Ok(val) = HeaderValue::from_str(\"true\") {\n            builder = builder.header(\"X-Miroir-Degraded\", val);\n        }\n    }\n\n    Ok(builder\n        .body(Json(search_response).into_response().into_body())\n        .unwrap())\n}\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use super::*;\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_search_request_deserialization() {\n assert_eq!(req.show_ranking_score, Some(true));\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_search_request_defaults() {\n        let json = r#\"{\"q\": \"test\"}\"#;\n\n        let req: SearchRequest = serde_json::from_str(json).unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(req.q, Some(\"test\".to_string()));\n        assert_eq!(req.limit, None);\n        assert_eq!(req.offset, None);\n        assert_eq!(req.show_ranking_score, None);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_search_response_serialization() {\n        let response = SearchResponse {\n            hits: result.responses.len() < quorum {\n        return Err(ErrorResponse::internal_error(\n            \"Failed to create index on quorum of nodes\",\n        ));\n    }\n\n    // Return first response\n multiple nodes.\nfn merge_field_distributions(distributions: Vec<Value>) -> Value {\n    use std::collections::HashMap;\n\n    let mut merged: HashMap<String, HashMap<String, u64>> = State(state): State<ProxyState>,\n340\t    Path((index, id)): Path<(String, String)>,\n341\t) -> Result<Response, ErrorResponse> {\n342\t    let topology = state.topology().await;\n343\t\n344\t    // ErrorResponse::internal_error(format!(\"Group {} not found\", group_id)))?;\n352\t\n353\t    let shard_id = shard_for_key(&id, state.config.shards);\n354\t    let rf = state.config.replication_factor as usize;\n355\t\n356\t    // Build covering set for this shard\n357\t    let covering = miroir_core::router::covering_set(1, group, rf, query_seq);\n358\t\n359\t    // Query the node responsible for this shard\n360\t    let target = covering\n361\t        .first()\n362\t        .ok_or_else(|| ErrorResponse::internal_error(\"No nodes in covering set\"))?;\n363\t\n364\t    let request = ScatterRequest {\n365\t        method: \"GET\".to_string(),\n366\t        path: format!(\"/indexes/{}/documents/{}\", index, id),\n367\t        body: vec![],\n368\t        headers: vec![],\n369\t    };\n370\t\n371\t    let scatter = HttpScatter::new((*state.client).clone(), state.config.server.request_timeout_ms);\n372\t    let result = scatter\n373\t        .scatter(&topology, vec![target.clone()], request, UnavailableShardPolicy::Error)\n374\t        .await\n375\t        .map_err(|e| ErrorResponse::internal_error(e.to_string()))?;\n376\t\n377\t    if from response\n384\t    let mut body = resp.body.clone();\n385\t    if let Some(obj) = body.as_object_mut() {\n386\t        obj.remove(\"_miroir_shard\");\n387\t    }\n388\t\n389\t    let status = StatusCode::from_u16(resp.status).unwrap_or(StatusCode::OK);\n390\t    Ok((status, Json(body)).into_response())\n391\t}\n392\t\n393\t/// Extract the primary key field from documents or headers.\n394\tfn get_primary_key(_documents: &[Value], headers: &HeaderMap) -> Option<String> {\n395\t    // Check for primary key in query string/header\n396\t    // For now, default to \"id\"\n397\t    // In production, we'd query the index settings\n398\t    if let Some(pk) = headers.get(\"X-Meiroil-Primary-Key\") {\n399\t        pk.to_str().ok().map(|s| s.to_string())\n400\t    } else {\n401\t        Some(\"id\".to_string())\n402\t    }\n403\t}\n404\t\n405\t#[cfg(test)]\n406\tmod tests {\n407\t    use super::*;\n408\t\n409\t    #[test]\n410\t    fn assert_eq!(pk, Some(\"user_id\".to_string()));\n424\t    }\n425\t}\n426\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"6586160f-073c-485b-bae7-4aeb044369a8","uuid":"1235cb3c-1bed-4190-8a44-8b83a53a404d","timestamp":"2026-05-09T14:41:03.880Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/home/coding/miroir/crates/miroir-proxy/src/routes/documents.rs","content":"//! Document routes: POST, PUT, DELETE, GET /documents\n//!\n//! Implements the write path per plan §2:\n//! - Hash primary key to get shard ID\n//! - Inject _miroir_shard field\n//! - Fan out to RG × RF nodes\n//! - Per-group quorum (floor(RF/2)+1)\n//! - X-Miroir-Degraded header on any group missing quorum\n//! - 503 miroir_no_quorum only when no group met quorum\n\nuse axum::{\n    extract::{Path, State},\n    http::{HeaderMap, HeaderValue, StatusCode},\n    response::{IntoResponse, Json, Response},\n};\nuse miroir_core::{\n    config::UnavailableShardPolicy,\n    merger::MergerImpl,\n    router::{shard_for_key, write_targets},\n    scatter::{Scatter, ScatterRequest},\n    topology::Topology,\n};\nuse serde_json::Value;\n\nuse crate::{\n    client::NodeClient,\n    error_response::ErrorResponse,\n    scatter::HttpScatter,\n    state::ProxyState,\n};\n\n/// Documents router.\npub fn router() -> axum::Router {\n    axum::Router::new()\n        .route(\"/:index\", axum::routing::post(add_documents))\n        .route(\"/:index/documents\", axum::routing::post(add_documents))\n        .route(\"/:index/documents\", axum::routing::put(update_documents))\n        .route(\"/:index/documents\", axum::routing::delete(delete_documents))\n        .route(\"/:index/documents/:id\", axum::routing::get(get_document))\n        .route(\"/:index/documents/:id\", axum::routing::delete(delete_document))\n}\n\n/// POST /:index/documents - Add or replace documents.\nasync fn add_documents(\n    State(state): State<ProxyState>,\n    Path(index): Path<String>,\n    headers: HeaderMap,\n    body: Vec<Value>,\n) -> Result<Response, ErrorResponse> {\n    let topology = state.topology().await;\n\n    // Get primary key for the index (for now, assume it's in the document or use default)\n    // In production, we'd query the index settings to get the primary key field\n    let primary_key = get_primary_key(&body, &headers).unwrap_or(\"id\");\n\n    // Inject _miroir_shard into each document and group by shard\n    let mut docs_by_shard: std::collections::HashMap<u32, Vec<Value>> = std::collections::HashMap::new();\n\n    for mut doc in body {\n        let pk_value = doc\n            .get(primary_key)\n            .and_then(|v| v.as_str())\n            .ok_or_else(|| ErrorResponse::invalid_request(format!(\"Missing primary key field '{primary_key}'\")))?;\n\n        let shard_id = shard_for_key(pk_value, state.config.shards);\n\n        // Inject _miroir_shard field\n        if let Some(obj) = doc.as_object_mut() {\n            obj.insert(\"_miroir_shard\".to_string(), Value::Number(shard_id.into()));\n        }\n\n        docs_by_shard.entry(shard_id).or_default().push(doc);\n    }\n\n    // For each shard, scatter write to all RG × RF nodes\n    let mut all_responses: Vec<Value> = Vec::new();\n    let mut any_degraded = false;\n    let mut any_success = false;\n\n    for (shard_id, docs) in docs_by_shard {\n        let targets = write_targets(shard_id, &topology);\n\n        if targets.is_empty() {\n            return Err(ErrorResponse::no_quorum(shard_id));\n        }\n\n        // Build request body\n        let body_bytes = serde_json::to_vec(&docs).unwrap_or_default();\n\n        let request = ScatterRequest {\n            method: \"POST\".to_string(),\n            path: format!(\"/indexes/{}/documents\", index),\n            body: body_bytes,\n            headers: vec![],\n        };\n\n        let scatter = HttpScatter::new((*state.client).clone(), state.config.server.request_timeout_ms);\n        let result = scatter\n            .scatter(&topology, targets, request, UnavailableShardPolicy::Partial)\n            .await\n            .map_err(|e| ErrorResponse::internal_error(e.to_string()))?;\n\n        // Check quorum per replica group\n        let rf = state.config.replication_factor as usize;\n        let quorum = (rf / 2) + 1;\n\n        // Group responses by replica group\n        let mut groups: std::collections::HashMap<u32, usize> = std::collections::HashMap::new();\n        for resp in &result.responses {\n            let node = topology.node(&resp.node_id).unwrap();\n            *groups.entry(node.replica_group).or_insert(0) += 1;\n        }\n\n        // Check if each group met quorum\n        for (group_id, count) in &groups {\n            if *count < quorum {\n                any_degraded = true;\n            } else {\n                any_success = true;\n            }\n        }\n\n        // Merge responses\n        for resp in result.responses {\n            all_responses.push(resp.body);\n        }\n    }\n\n    // If no group met quorum, return 503\n    if !any_success {\n        return Err(ErrorResponse::no_quorum(0));\n    }\n\n    // Build response\n    let task_uid = 1; // TODO: proper task ID generation\n    let mut response_body = serde_json::json!({\n        \"taskUid\": task_uid,\n        \"indexUid\": index,\n        \"status\": \"enqueued\",\n        \"type\": \"documentAdditionOrUpdate\",\n        \"enqueuedAt\": chrono::Utc::now().to_rfc3339(),\n    });\n\n    let mut builder = Response::builder().status(202);\n\n    // Add degraded header if any group was degraded\n    if any_degraded {\n        if let Ok(val) = HeaderValue::from_str(\"true\") {\n            builder = builder.header(\"X-Miroir-Degraded\", val);\n        }\n    }\n\n    Ok(builder.body(Json(response_body).into_response().into_body()).unwrap())\n}\n\n/// PUT /:index/documents - Update documents.\nasync fn update_documents(\n    State(state): State<ProxyState>,\n    Path(index): Path<String>,\n    headers: HeaderMap,\n    body: Vec<Value>,\n) -> Result<Response, ErrorResponse> {\n    // Same logic as POST, just different type\n    add_documents(state, Path(index), headers, body).await\n}\n\n/// DELETE /:index/documents - Delete documents by batch.\nasync fn delete_documents(\n    State(state): State<ProxyState>,\n    Path(index): Path<String>,\n    body: Value,\n) -> Result<Response, ErrorResponse> {\n    let topology = state.topology().await;\n\n    // Extract filter or IDs from request body\n    let ids = body\n        .get(\"ids\")\n        .and_then(|v| v.as_array())\n        .ok_or_else(|| ErrorResponse::invalid_request(\"Missing 'ids' field in delete request\"))?;\n\n    // Group by shard\n    let mut docs_by_shard: std::collections::HashMap<u32, Vec<String>> = std::collections::HashMap::new();\n\n    for id_val in ids {\n        let id = id_val\n            .as_str()\n            .ok_or_else(|| ErrorResponse::invalid_request(\"ID must be a string\"))?;\n\n        let shard_id = shard_for_key(id, state.config.shards);\n        docs_by_shard.entry(shard_id).or_default().push(id.to_string());\n    }\n\n    // For each shard, scatter delete to all RG × RF nodes\n    let mut any_degraded = false;\n    let mut any_success = false;\n\n    for (shard_id, ids) in docs_by_shard {\n        let targets = write_targets(shard_id, &topology);\n\n        if targets.is_empty() {\n            return Err(ErrorResponse::no_quorum(shard_id));\n        }\n\n        let body_bytes = serde_json::to_vec(&serde_json::json!({ \"ids\": ids })).unwrap_or_default();\n\n        let request = ScatterRequest {\n            method: \"POST\".to_string(),\n            path: format!(\"/indexes/{}/documents/delete\", index),\n            body: body_bytes,\n            headers: vec![],\n        };\n\n        let scatter = HttpScatter::new((*state.client).clone(), state.config.server.request_timeout_ms);\n        let result = scatter\n            .scatter(&topology, targets, request, UnavailableShardPolicy::Partial)\n            .await\n            .map_err(|e| ErrorResponse::internal_error(e.to_string()))?;\n\n        // Check quorum per replica group\n        let rf = state.config.replication_factor as usize;\n        let quorum = (rf / 2) + 1;\n\n        let mut groups: std::collections::HashMap<u32, usize> = std::collections::HashMap::new();\n        for resp in &result.responses {\n            let node = topology.node(&resp.node_id).unwrap();\n            *groups.entry(node.replica_group).or_insert(0) += 1;\n        }\n\n        for (group_id, count) in &groups {\n            if *count < quorum {\n                any_degraded = true;\n            } else {\n                any_success = true;\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    if !any_success {\n        return Err(ErrorResponse::no_quorum(0));\n    }\n\n    let task_uid = 1;\n    let mut response_body = serde_json::json!({\n        \"taskUid\": task_uid,\n        \"indexUid\": index,\n        \"status\": \"enqueued\",\n        \"type\": \"documentDeletion\",\n        \"enqueuedAt\": chrono::Utc::now().to_rfc3339(),\n    });\n\n    let mut builder = Response::builder().status(202);\n\n    if any_degraded {\n        if let Ok(val) = HeaderValue::from_str(\"true\") {\n            builder = builder.header(\"X-Miroir-Degraded\", val);\n        }\n    }\n\n State<ProxyState>,\n    Path((index, id)): Path<(String, String)>,\n) -> Result<Response, ErrorResponse> {\n    let topology = state.topology().await;\n\n    let shard_id = shard_for_key(&id, state.config.shards);\n    let targets = write_targets(shard_id, &topology);\n\n    if targets.is_empty() {\n        return Err(ErrorResponse::no_quorum(shard_id));\n    }\n\n    let body_bytes =\n        serde_json::to_vec(&serde_json::json!({ \"ids\": [id] })).unwrap_or_default();\n\n    let request = ScatterRequest {\n        method: \"POST\".to_string(),\n        path: format!(\"/indexes/{}/documents/delete\", index),\n        body: body_bytes,\n        headers: vec![],\n    };\n\n    let scatter = HttpScatter::new((*state.client).clone(), state.config.server.request_timeout_ms);\n    let result = scatter\n        .scatter(&topology, targets, request, UnavailableShardPolicy::Partial)\n        .await\n        .map_err(|e| ErrorResponse::internal_error(e.to_string()))?;\n\n    // Check quorum\n    let rf = state.config.replication_factor as usize;\n    let quorum = (rf / 2) + 1;\n\n    let mut groups: std::collections::HashMap<u32, usize> = std::collections::HashMap::new();\n    for resp in &result.responses {\n        let node = topology.node(&resp.node_id).unwrap();\n        *groups.entry(node.replica_group).or_insert(0) += 1;\n    }\n\n    let mut any_degraded = false;\n    let mut any_success = false;\n\n    for (_group_id, count) in &groups {\n        if *count < quorum {\n            any_degraded = true;\n        } else {\n            any_success = true;\n        }\n    }\n\n    if !any_success {\n        return Err(ErrorResponse::no_quorum(shard_id));\n    }\n\n    let task_uid = 1;\n    let mut response_body = serde_json::json!({\n        \"taskUid\": task_uid,\n        \"indexUid\": index,\n        \"status\": \"enqueued\",\n        \"type\": \"documentDeletion\",\n        \"enqueuedAt\": chrono::Utc::now().to_rfc3339(),\n    });\n\n    let mut builder = Response::builder().status(202);\n\n    if any_degraded {\n        if let Ok(val) = HeaderValue::from_str(\"true\") {\n            builder = builder.header(\"X-Miroir-Degraded\", val);\n        }\n    }\n\n    Ok(builder.body(Json(response_body).into_response().into_body()).unwrap())\n}\n\n/// GET /:index/documents/:id - Get a single document by ID.\nasync fn get_document(\n    State(state): State<ProxyState>,\n    Path((index, id)): Path<(String, String)>,\n) -> Result<Response, ErrorResponse> {\n    let topology = state.topology().await;\n\n    // For GET, we only need to query one replica group\n    // Use the query group (round-robin)\n    let query_seq = state.next_query_seq();\n    let group_id = miroir_core::router::query_group(query_seq, state.config.replica_groups);\n\n state.config.shards);\n    let rf = state.config.replication_factor as usize;\n\n    // Build covering set for this shard\n    let covering = miroir_core::router::covering_set(1, group, rf, query_seq);\n\n    // Query the node responsible for this shard\n    let target = covering\n        .first()\n        .ok_or_else(|| ErrorResponse::internal_error(\"No nodes in covering set\"))?;\n\n    let request = ScatterRequest {\n        method: \"GET\".to_string(),\n        path: format!(\"/indexes/{}/documents/{}\", index, id),\n        body: vec![],\n        headers: vec![],\n    };\n\n UnavailableShardPolicy::Error)\n        .await\n        .map_err(|e| ErrorResponse::internal_error(e.to_string()))?;\n\n    if result.responses.is_empty() {\n        return Err(ErrorResponse::document_not_found(&id));\n    }\n\n    let resp = &result.responses[0];\n\n    // Strip _miroir_shard from response\n    let mut body = resp.body.clone();\n    if let Some(obj) = body.as_object_mut() {\n        obj.remove(\"_miroir_shard\");\n    }\n\n    let status = StatusCode::from_u16(resp.status).unwrap_or(StatusCode::OK);\n    Ok((status, Json(body)).into_response())\n}\n\n/// Extract the primary key field from documents or headers.\nfn get_primary_key(_documents: &[Value], headers: &HeaderMap) -> Option<String> {\n    // Check for primary key in query string/header\n    // For now, default to \"id\"\n    // In production, we'd query the index settings\n    if let Some(pk) = headers.get(\"X-Meiroil-Primary-Key\") {\n        pk.to_str().ok().map(|s| s.to_string())\n    } else {\n        Some(\"id\".to_string())\n    }\n}\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use super::*;\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_get_primary_key_default()         headers: vec![],\n            };\n\n            let scatter = HttpScatter::new((*state.client).clone(), state.config.server.request_timeout_ms);\n\n            match scatter\n                .scatter(&topology, vec![node_id.clone()], request, UnavailableShardPolicy::Partial)\n                .await\n            {\n                Ok(result) => {\n                    if let Some(resp) =                 for task_value in results {\n                                    if let Ok(task) = serde_json::from_value::<NodeTask>(task_value.clone()) {\n                                        all_tasks.push(task);\n                                    }\n                                }\n                            }\n                        }\n                    }\n                }\n .filter(|t| index_uids.contains(&t.index_uid))\n                .collect();\n        }\n    }\n\n    if let Some(statuses) = &query.statuses {\n        if !statuses.is_empty() {\n            filtered_tasks = filtered_tasks\n                .into_iter()\n                .filter(|t| statuses.contains(&t.status))\n                .collect();\n        }\n    }\n\n    if let Some(types) = &query.types {\n        if !types.is_empty() {\n            filtered_tasks = filtered_tasks\n                .into_iter()\n                .filter(|t| types.contains(&t.task_type))\n                .collect();\n        }\n    }\n\n    // Aggregate tasks by UID\n    let mut aggregated: std::collections::HashMap<u32, Vec<NodeTask>> = std::collections::HashMap::new();\n\n    for task in filtered_tasks {\n        aggregated\n            .entry(task.task_uid as u32)\n            .or_insert_with(Vec::new)\n            .push(task);\n    }\n\n    // Convert to aggregated tasks\n    let mut results: Vec<AggregatedTask> = aggregated\n        .into_iter()\n        .map(|(uid, tasks)| {\n            let first = tasks.first().unwrap();\n\n            // Determine overall status\n            let status = if tasks.iter().any(|t| t.status == \"failed\") {\n                \"failed\".to_string()\n            } else if tasks.iter().any(|t| t.status == \"processing\") {\n                \"processing\".to_string()\n            } else if tasks.iter().any(|t| t.status == \"enqueued\") {\n                \"enqueued\".to_string()\n            } else {\n                \"succeeded\".to_string()\n            };\n\n            let nodes_completed = tasks.iter().filter(|t| t.status == \"succeeded\").count() as u32;\n            let nodes_failed = tasks.iter().filter(|t| t.status == \"failed\").count() as u32;\n\n            AggregatedTask {\n                task_uid: uid as u64,\n                index_uid: first.index_uid.clone(),\n                status,\n                task_type: first.task_type.clone(),\n                enqueued_at: first.enqueued_at.clone(),\n                started_at: first.started_at.clone(),\n                finished_at: first.finished_at.clone(),\n                error: first.error.clone(),\n                details: first.details.clone(),\n                duration: first.duration.clone(),\n                node_count: tasks.len() as u32,\n                nodes_completed,\n                nodes_failed,\n            }\n        })\n        .collect();\n\n    // Sort by task UID descending\n    results.sort_by(|a, b| b.task_uid.cmp(&a.task_uid));\n\n    // Apply from/limit pagination\n    let total = results.len() as u32;\n    if let Some(from_uid) = from {\n        results = results.into_iter().filter(|t| t.task_uid <= from_uid as u64).collect();\n    }\n    results.truncate(limit);\n\n    Ok(Json(TasksListResponse {\n        results,\n        limit,\n        from,\n        total,\n    }))\n}\n\n/// GET /tasks/:uid - Get a specific task.\nasync fn get_task(\n    State(state): State<ProxyState>,\n    Path(uid): Path<String>,\n) -> Result<Response, ErrorResponse> {\n    let topology = state.topology().await;\n\n    // Parse task UID\n    let task_uid = uid\n        .parse::<u64>()\n        .map_err(|_| ErrorResponse::invalid_request(\"Invalid task UID\"))?;\n\n    // Query all nodes for this task\n    let mut node_tasks: Vec<NodeTask> = Vec::new();\n    let mut not_found = true;\n\n    for group in topology.groups() {\n        if let Some(node_id) = group.nodes().first() {\n            let request = ScatterRequest {\n                method: \"GET\".to_string(),\n                path: format!(\"/tasks/{}\", task_uid),\n                body: vec![],\n                headers: vec![],\n            };\n\n            let scatter = HttpScatter::new((*state.client).clone(), state.config.server.request_timeout_ms);\n\n            if let Ok(result) = scatter\n                .scatter(&topology, vec![node_id.clone()], request, UnavailableShardPolicy::Partial)\n                .await\n            {\n                if let Some(resp) = result.responses.first() {\n                    if resp.status == 200 {\n                        not_found = false;\n                        if let Ok(task) = serde_json::from_value::<NodeTask>(resp.body.clone()) {\n                            node_tasks.push(task);\n                        }\n                    } else if resp.status == 404 {\n                        // Task not found on this node\n                    }\n                }\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    if not_found {\n        return Err(ErrorResponse::invalid_request(format!(\"Task {} not found\", uid)));\n    }\n\n    if node_tasks.is_empty() {\n        return Err(ErrorResponse::invalid_request(format!(\"Task {} not found\", uid)));\n    }\n\n    // Aggregate task status\n    let first = node_tasks.first().unwrap();\n    let status = if node_tasks.iter().any(|t| t.status == \"failed\") {\n        \"failed\".to_string()\n    } else if node_tasks.iter().any(|t| t.status == \"processing\") {\n        \"processing\".to_string()\n    } else if node_tasks.iter().any(|t| t.status == \"enqueued\") {\n        \"enqueued\".to_string()\n    } else {\n        \"succeeded\".to_string()\n    };\n\n    let nodes_completed = node_tasks.iter().filter(|t| t.status == \"succeeded\").count() as u32;\n    let nodes_failed = node_tasks.iter().filter(|t| t.status == \"failed\").count() as u32;\n\n    let aggregated = AggregatedTask {\n        task_uid: first.task_uid,\n        index_uid: first.index_uid.clone(),\n        status,\n        task_type: first.task_type.clone(),\n        enqueued_at: first.enqueued_at.clone(),\n        started_at: first.started_at.clone(),\n        finished_at: first.finished_at.clone(),\n        error: first.error.clone(),\n        details: first.details.clone(),\n        duration: first.duration.clone(),\n        node_count: node_tasks.len() as u32,\n        nodes_completed,\n        nodes_failed,\n    };\n\n    Ok((axum::http::StatusCode::OK, Json(aggregated)).into_response())\n}\n\n/// DELETE /tasks/:uid - Cancel/delete a task.\nasync let task_uid = uid\n        .parse::<u64>()\n        .map_err(|_| ErrorResponse::invalid_request(\"Invalid task UID\"))?;\n\n    // Broadcast delete to all nodes\n    let targets = write_targets(0, &topology);\n\n    if targets.is_empty() {\n        return Err(ErrorResponse::internal_error(\"No nodes available\"));\n    }\n\n    let request = ScatterRequest {\n        method: Scatter orchestration: fan-out logic and covering set builder.\n\nuse async_trait::async_trait;\nuse crate::config::UnavailableShardPolicy;\nuse crate::topology::{NodeId, Topology};\nuse crate::Result;\n\n/// Scatter orchestrator: fans out requests to the covering set.\n#[async_trait]\npub trait Scatter: Send + Sync {\n    /// Execute a scatter request to multiple nodes.\n    ///\n    /// Returns a map of node ID to response. Failed nodes are omitted\n    /// based on the unavailable shard policy.\n    async fn scatter(\n        &self,\n        topology: &Topology,\n        nodes: Vec<NodeId>,\n        request: ScatterRequest,\n        policy: UnavailableShardPolicy,\n    ) -> Result<ScatterResponse>;\n}\n\n/// A scatter request to be sent to each node.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\npub struct ScatterRequest {\n    /// Request body (JSON or raw bytes).\n    pub body: Vec<u8>,\n\n    /// Request headers.\n    pub headers: Vec<(String, String)>,\n\n    /// HTTP method.\n    pub method: String,\n\n    /// Request path.\n    pub path: String,\n}\n\n/// Response from a scatter operation.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\npub struct ScatterResponse {\n    /// Responses from successful nodes.\n    pub responses: Vec<NodeResponse>,\n\n    /// Nodes that failed or timed out.\n    pub failed: Vec<NodeId>,\n}\n\n/// Response from a single node.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\npub struct NodeResponse {\n    /// Node that responded.\n    pub node_id: NodeId,\n\n    /// Response body.\n    pub body: Vec<u8>,\n\n    /// HTTP status code.\n    pub status: u16,\n\n    /// Response headers.\n    pub headers: Vec<(String, String)>,\n}\n\n/// Default stub implementation of Scatter.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Default)]\npub struct StubScatter;\n\n#[async_trait]\nimpl Scatter for StubScatter {\n    async fn scatter(\n        &self,\n        _topology: &Topology,\n        _nodes: Vec<NodeId>,\n        _request: ScatterRequest,\n        _policy: UnavailableShardPolicy,\n    ) -> Result<ScatterResponse> {\n        Ok(ScatterResponse {\n            responses: Vec::new(),\n            failed: Vec::new(),\n        })\n    }\n}\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use super::*;\n    use crate::config::UnavailableShardPolicy;\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_scatter_request_creation() {\n        let request = ScatterRequest {\n            body: b\"test body\".to_vec(),\n            headers: vec![(\"Content-Type\".to_string(), \"application/json\".to_string())],\n            method: \"POST\".to_string(),\n            path: \"/search\".to_string(),\n        };\n\n        assert_eq!(request.body, b\"test body\");\n        assert_eq!(request.headers.len(), 1);\n        assert_eq!(request.method, \"POST\");\n        assert_eq!(request.path, \"/search\");\n    }\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn test_stub_scatter_returns_empty_response() {\n        let scatter = StubScatter;\n        let topology = Topology::new(1);\n        let nodes = vec![NodeId::new(\"node1\".to_string())];\n        let request = ScatterRequest {\n            body: Vec::new(),\n            headers: Vec::new(),\n            method: \"GET\".to_string(),\n            path: \"/\".to_string(),\n        };\n\n        let result = scatter\n            .scatter(&topology, nodes, request, UnavailableShardPolicy::Partial)\n            .await\n            .unwrap();\n\n        assert!(result.responses.is_empty());\n        assert!(result.failed.is_empty());\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_scatter_response_empty() {\n        let response = ScatterResponse {\n            responses: Vec::new(),\n            failed: Vec::new(),\n        };\n\n        assert!(response.responses.is_empty());\n        assert!(response.failed.is_empty());\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_scatter_response_with_data() {\n        let node_response = NodeResponse {\n            node_id: NodeId::new(\"node1\".to_string()),\n            body: b\"response\".to_vec(),\n            status: 200,\n            headers: vec![(\"Content-Type\".to_string(), \"application/json\".to_string())],\n        };\n\n        let response = ScatterResponse {\n            responses: vec![node_response],\n            failed: vec![NodeId::new(\"node2\".to_string())],\n        };\n\n        assert_eq!(response.responses.len(), 1);\n        assert_eq!(response.failed.len(), 1);\n        assert_eq!(response.responses[0].node_id.as_str(), \"node1\");\n        assert_eq!(response.responses[0].status, 200);\n        assert_eq!(response.failed[0].as_str(), \"node2\");\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_node_response_creation() {\n        let response = NodeResponse {\n            node_id: NodeId::new(\"test-node\".to_string()),\n            body: b\"test body\".to_vec(),\n            status: 200,\n            headers: vec![(\"X-Custom\".to_string(), \"value\".to_string())],\n        };\n\n        assert_eq!(response.node_id.as_str(), \"test-node\");\n        assert_eq!(response.body, b\"test body\");\n        assert_eq!(response.status, 200);\n        assert_eq!(response.headers.len(), 1);\n        assert_eq!(response.headers[0].0, \"X-Custom\");\n assert!(result.responses.is_empty());\n        assert!(result.failed.is_empty());\n    }\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn test_stub_scatter_with_multiple_nodes() {\n        let scatter = {"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_9ddf1ecb898d454fbcc028fc","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t//! Result merger: combines shard results into a single response.\n2\t\n3\tuse crate::Result;\n4\tuse serde_json::Value;\n5\tuse std::collections::HashMap;\n6\t\n7\t/// Result merger: combines responses from multiple shards.\n8\tpub trait Merger: Send + Sync {\n9\t    /// Merge search results from multiple shards.\n10\t    ///\n11\t    /// Takes the raw JSON responses from each shard and produces\n12\t    /// a merged result with global sorting, offset/limit applied,\n13\t    /// and facet aggregation.\n14\t    fn merge(\n15\t        &self,\n16\t        shard_responses: Vec<ShardResponse>,\n17\t        offset: usize,\n18\t        limit: usize,\n19\t        client_requested_score: bool,\n20\t    ) -> Result<MergedResult>;\n21\t}\n22\t\n23\t/// Response from a single shard.\n24\t#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\n25\tpub struct ShardResponse {\n26\t    /// Shard identifier.\n27\t    pub shard_id: u32,\n28\t\n29\t    /// Raw JSON response from the node.\n30\t    pub body: Value,\n31\t\n32\t    /// Whether this shard succeeded.\n33\t    pub success: bool,\n34\t}\n35\t\n36\t/// Merged search result.\n37\t#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\n38\tpub struct MergedResult {\n39\t    /// Merged hits (globally sorted, offset/limit applied).\n40\t    pub hits: Vec<Value>,\n41\t\n42\t    /// Aggregated facets.\n43\t    pub facets: Value,\n44\t\n45\t    /// Estimated total hits (sum of shard totals).\n46\t    pub total_hits: u64,\n47\t\n48\t    /// Processing time in milliseconds.\n49\t    pub processing_time_ms: u64,\n50\t\n51\t    /// Whether the response is degraded (some shards failed).\n52\t    pub degraded: bool,\n53\t}\n54\t\n55\t/// Default implementation of Merger.\n56\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Default)]\n57\tpub struct MergerImpl;\n58\t\n59\timpl Merger for MergerImpl {\n60\t    fn merge(\n61\t        &self,\n62\t        shard_responses: Vec<ShardResponse>,\n63\t        offset: usize,\n64\t        limit: usize,\n65\t        client_requested_score: bool,\n66\t    ) -> Result<MergedResult> {\n67\t        // Filter to only successful responses\n68\t        let successful_shards: Vec<_> = shard_responses.iter().filter(|s| s.success).collect();\n69\t\n70\t        // Check if any shards failed (degraded mode)\n71\t        let degraded = successful_shards.len() < shard_responses.len();\n72\t\n73\t        // Collect all hits with their ranking scores\n74\t        let mut all_hits: Vec<(f64, Value)> = Vec::new();\n75\t\n76\t        for shard in &successful_shards {\n77\t            if let Some(hits) = shard.body.get(\"hits\").and_then(|h| h.as_array()) {\n78\t                for hit in hits {\n79\t                    // Extract ranking score\n80\t                    let score = hit\n81\t                        .get(\"_rankingScore\")\n82\t                        .and_then(|s| s.as_f64())\n83\t                        .unwrap_or(0.0);\n84\t\n85\t                    all_hits.push((score, hit.clone()));\n86\t                }\n87\t            }\n88\t        }\n89\t\n90\t        // Sort globally by score descending\n91\t        all_hits.sort_by(|a, b| b.0.partial_cmp(&a.0).unwrap_or(std::cmp::Ordering::Equal));\n92\t\n93\t        // Apply offset and limit\n94\t        let page: Vec<Value> = all_hits\n95\t            .into_iter()\n96\t            .skip(offset)\n97\t            .take(limit)\n98\t            .map(|(_, mut hit)| {\n99\t                // Strip _miroir_shard (always removed)\n100\t                if let Some(obj) = hit.as_object_mut() {\n101\t                    obj.remove(\"_miroir_shard\");\n102\t                }\n103\t\n104\t                // Strip _rankingScore if client didn't request it\n105\t                if !client_requested_score {\n106\t                    if let Some(obj) = hit.as_object_mut() {\n107\t                        obj.remove(\"_rankingScore\");\n108\t                    }\n109\t                }\n110\t\n111\t                hit\n112\t            })\n113\t            .collect();\n114\t\n115\t        // Aggregate facets across all shards\n116\t        let facets = merge_facets(&successful_shards);\n117\t\n118\t        // Sum estimated total hits\n119\t        let total_hits: u64 = successful_shards\n120\t            .iter()\n121\t            .filter_map(|s| s.body.get(\"estimatedTotalHits\").and_then(|v| v.as_u64()))\n122\t            .sum();\n123\t\n124\t        // Max processing time across all shards\n125\t        let processing_time_ms: u64 = successful_shards\n126\t            .iter()\n127\t            .filter_map(|s| s.body.get(\"processingTimeMs\").and_then(|v| v.as_u64()))\n128\t            .max()\n129\t            .unwrap_or(0);\n130\t\n131\t        Ok(MergedResult {\n132\t            hits: page,\n133\t            facets,\n134\t            total_hits,\n135\t            processing_time_ms,\n136\t            degraded,\n137\t        })\n138\t    }\n139\t}\n140\t\n141\t/// Merge facet distributions from all shards.\n142\t///\n143\t/// Facets are nested objects like `{\"color\": {\"red\": 10, \"blue\": 5}}`.\n144\t/// We sum counts for each facet value across all shards.\n145\tfn merge_facets(shards: &[&ShardResponse]) -> Value {\n146\t    let mut merged_facets: HashMap<String, HashMap<String, u64>> = HashMap::new();\n147\t\n148\t    for shard in shards {\n149\t        if let Some(facets) = shard\n150\t            .body\n151\t            .get(\"facetDistribution\")\n152\t            .and_then(|f| f.as_object())\n153\t        {\n154\t            for (facet_name, facet_values) in facets {\n155\t                if let Some(values_obj) = facet_values.as_object() {\n156\t                    let entry = merged_facets.entry(facet_name.clone()).or_default();\n157\t\n158\t                    for (value, count) in values_obj {\n159\t                        let count_val = count.as_u64().unwrap_or(0);\n160\t                        *entry.entry(value.clone()).or_insert(0) += count_val;\n161\t                    }\n162\t                }\n163\t            }\n164\t        }\n165\t    }\n166\t\n167\t    // Convert back to JSON structure\n168\t    let mut result = serde_json::Map::new();\n169\t    for (facet_name, values) in merged_facets {\n170\t        let values_obj: serde_json::Map<String, Value> = values\n171\t            .into_iter()\n172\t            .map(|(k, v)| (k, Value::Number(v.into())))\n173\t            .collect();\n174\t        result.insert(facet_name, Value::Object(values_obj));\n175\t    }\n176\t\n177\t    Value::Object(result)\n178\t}\n179\t\n180\t/// Stub implementation that returns empty results.\n181\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Default)]\n182\tpub struct StubMerger;\n183\t\n184\timpl Merger for StubMerger {\n185\t    fn merge(\n186\t        &self,\n187\t        _shard_responses: Vec<ShardResponse>,\n188\t        _offset: usize,\n189\t        _limit: usize,\n190\t        _client_requested_score: bool,\n191\t    ) -> Result<MergedResult> {\n192\t        Ok(MergedResult {\n193\t            hits: Vec::new(),\n194\t            facets: serde_json::json!({}),\n195\t            total_hits: 0,\n196\t            processing_time_ms: 0,\n197\t            degraded: false,\n198\t        })\n199\t    }\n200\t}\n201\t\n202\t#[cfg(test)]\n203\tmod tests {\n204\t    use super::*;\n205\t\n206\t    fn create_hit(score: f64, id: &str) -> Value {\n207\t        serde_json::json!({\n208\t            \"id\": id,\n209\t            \"_rankingScore\": score,\n210\t            \"_miroir_shard\": 0,\n211\t        })\n212\t    }\n213\t\n214\t    fn create_shard_response(shard_id: u32, hits: Vec<Value>, total: u64) -> ShardResponse {\n215\t        ShardResponse {\n216\t            shard_id,\n217\t            body: serde_json::json!({\n218\t                \"hits\": hits,\n219\t                \"estimatedTotalHits\": total,\n220\t                \"processingTimeMs\": test_global_sort_by_ranking_score() {\n229\t        let merger = MergerImpl;\n230\t\n231\t        let hits1 = vec![\n232\t            create_hit(0.5, \"doc1\"),\n233\t            create_hit(0.9, \"doc2\"),\n234\t            create_hit(0.3, \"doc3\"),\n235\t        ];\n236\t        let hits2 = vec![\n237\t            create_hit(0.7, \"doc4\"),\n238\t            create_hit(0.1, \"doc5\"),\n239\t            create_hit(0.8, \"doc6\"),\n240\t        ];\n241\t\n242\t        let shards = vec![\n243\t            create_shard_response(0, hits1, 3),\n244\t            create_shard_response(1, hits2, 3),\n245\t        ];\n246\t\n247\t        let result = merger.merge(shards, 0, 10, false).unwrap();\n248\t\n249\t        // Check global ordering: should be doc2(0.9), doc6(0.8), doc4(0.7), doc1(0.5), doc3(0.3), doc5(0.1)\n250\t        assert_eq!(result.hits.len(), 6);\n251\t        assert_eq!(result.hits[0][\"id\"], \"doc2\");\n252\t        assert_eq!(result.hits[1][\"id\"], \"doc6\");\n253\t        assert_eq!(result.hits[2][\"id\"], \"doc4\");\n254\t        assert_eq!(result.hits[3][\"id\"], \"doc1\");\n255\t        assert_eq!(result.hits[4][\"id\"], \"doc3\");\n256\t        assert_eq!(result.hits[5][\"id\"], \"doc5\");\n257\t    }\n258\t\n259\t    #[test]\n260\t    fn test_offset_and_limit_applied_after_merge() {\n261\t        let merger = MergerImpl;\n262\t\n263\t        let hits = vec![\n264\t            create_hit(0.9, \"doc1\"),\n265\t            create_hit(0.8, \"doc2\"),\n266\t            create_hit(0.7, \"doc3\"),\n267\t            create_hit(0.6, \"doc4\"),\n268\t            create_hit(0.5, \"doc5\"),\n269\t        ];\n270\t\n271\t        let shards = vec![create_shard_response(0,        assert_eq!(result.hits[0][\"id\"], \"doc3\");\n278\t        assert_eq!(result.hits[1][\"id\"], \"doc4\");\n279\t    }\n280\t\n281\t    #[test]\n282\t    fn   let shards = vec![create_shard_response(0, hits, 2)];\n288\t\n289\t        let result = merger.merge(shards, 0, 10, false).unwrap();\n290\t\n291\t        // _rankingScore should be stripped\n292\t        assert!(result.hits[0].get(\"_rankingScore\").is_none());\n293\t        assert!(result.hits[1].get(\"_rankingScore\").is_none());\n294\t    }\n295\t\n296\t    #[test]\n297\t    fn    #[test]\n596\t    fn test_limit_exceeds_available_hits() {\n597\t        let merger = MergerImpl;\n598\t\n599\t        let hits = vec![create_hit(0.9, \"doc1\"), create_hit(0.8,
