assert_eq!(result.hits.len(), 2);\n606\t    }\n607\t}\n608\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"6586160f-073c-485b-bae7-4aeb044369a8","uuid":"65551c35-c1da-48f2-9e73-2d2a67e6fa12","timestamp":"2026-05-09T14:41:17.027Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/home/coding/miroir/crates/miroir-core/src/merger.rs","content":"//! Result merger: combines shard results into a single response.\n\nuse crate::Result;\nuse serde_json::Value;\nuse std::collections::HashMap;\n\n/// Result merger: combines responses from multiple shards.\npub trait Merger: Send + Sync {\n    /// Merge search results from multiple shards.\n    ///\n    /// Takes the raw JSON responses from each shard and produces\n    /// a merged result with global sorting, offset/limit applied,\n    /// and facet aggregation.\n    fn merge(\n        &self,\n        shard_responses: Vec<ShardResponse>,\n        offset: usize,\n        limit: usize,\n        client_requested_score: bool,\n    ) -> Result<MergedResult>;\n}\n\n/// Response from a single shard.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\npub struct ShardResponse {\n    /// Shard identifier.\n    pub shard_id: u32,\n\n    /// Raw JSON response from the node.\n    pub body: Value,\n\n    /// Whether this shard succeeded.\n    pub success: bool,\n}\n\n/// Merged search result.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\npub struct MergedResult {\n    /// Merged hits (globally sorted, offset/limit applied).\n    pub hits: Vec<Value>,\n\n    /// Aggregated facets.\n    pub facets: Value,\n\n    /// Estimated total hits (sum of shard totals).\n    pub total_hits: u64,\n\n    /// Processing time in milliseconds.\n    pub processing_time_ms: u64,\n\n    /// Whether the response is degraded (some shards failed).\n    pub degraded: bool,\n}\n\n/// Default implementation of Merger.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Default)]\npub struct MergerImpl;\n\nimpl Merger for MergerImpl {\n    fn merge(\n        &self,\n        shard_responses: Vec<ShardResponse>,\n        offset: usize,\n        limit: usize,\n        client_requested_score: bool,\n    ) -> Result<MergedResult> {\n        // Filter to only successful responses\n        let successful_shards: Vec<_> = shard_responses.iter().filter(|s| s.success).collect();\n\n        // Check if any shards failed (degraded mode)\n        let degraded = successful_shards.len() < shard_responses.len();\n\n        // Collect all hits with their ranking scores\n        let mut all_hits: Vec<(f64, Value)> = Vec::new();\n\n        for shard in &successful_shards {\n            if let Some(hits) = shard.body.get(\"hits\").and_then(|h| h.as_array()) {\n                for hit in hits {\n                    // Extract ranking score\n                    let score = hit\n                        .get(\"_rankingScore\")\n                        .and_then(|s| s.as_f64())\n                        .unwrap_or(0.0);\n\n                    all_hits.push((score, hit.clone()));\n                }\n            }\n        }\n\n        // Sort globally by score descending\n        all_hits.sort_by(|a, b| b.0.partial_cmp(&a.0).unwrap_or(std::cmp::Ordering::Equal));\n\n        // Apply offset and limit\n        let page: Vec<Value> = all_hits\n            .into_iter()\n            .skip(offset)\n            .take(limit)\n            .map(|(_, mut hit)| {\n                // Strip _miroir_shard (always removed)\n                if let Some(obj) = hit.as_object_mut() {\n                    obj.remove(\"_miroir_shard\");\n                }\n\n                // Strip _rankingScore if client didn't request it\n                if !client_requested_score {\n                    if let Some(obj) = hit.as_object_mut() {\n                        obj.remove(\"_rankingScore\");\n                    }\n                }\n\n                hit\n            })\n            .collect();\n\n        // Aggregate facets across all shards\n        let facets = merge_facets(&successful_shards);\n\n        // Sum estimated total hits\n        let total_hits: u64 = successful_shards\n            .iter()\n            .filter_map(|s| s.body.get(\"estimatedTotalHits\").and_then(|v| v.as_u64()))\n            .sum();\n\n        // Max processing time across all shards\n        let processing_time_ms: u64 = successful_shards\n            .iter()\n            .filter_map(|s| s.body.get(\"processingTimeMs\").and_then(|v| v.as_u64()))\n            .max()\n            .unwrap_or(0);\n\n        Ok(MergedResult {\n            hits: page,\n            facets,\n            total_hits,\n            processing_time_ms,\n            degraded,\n        })\n    }\n}\n\n/// Merge facet distributions from all shards.\n///\n/// Facets are nested objects like `{\"color\": {\"red\": 10, \"blue\": 5}}`.\n/// We sum counts for each facet value across all shards.\nfn merge_facets(shards: &[&ShardResponse]) -> Value {\n    let mut merged_facets: HashMap<String, HashMap<String, u64>> = HashMap::new();\n\n    for shard in shards {\n        if let Some(facets) = shard\n            .body\n            .get(\"facetDistribution\")\n            .and_then(|f| f.as_object())\n        {\n            for (facet_name, facet_values) in facets {\n                if let Some(values_obj) = facet_values.as_object() {\n                    let entry = merged_facets.entry(facet_name.clone()).or_default();\n\n                    for (value, count) in values_obj {\n                        let count_val = count.as_u64().unwrap_or(0);\n                        *entry.entry(value.clone()).or_insert(0) += count_val;\n                    }\n                }\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    // Convert back to JSON structure\n    let mut result = serde_json::Map::new();\n    for (facet_name, values) in merged_facets {\n        let values_obj: serde_json::Map<String, Value> = values\n            .into_iter()\n            .map(|(k, v)| (k, Value::Number(v.into())))\n            .collect();\n        result.insert(facet_name, Value::Object(values_obj));\n    }\n\n    Value::Object(result)\n}\n\n/// Stub implementation that returns empty results.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Default)]\npub struct StubMerger;\n\nimpl Merger for StubMerger {\n    fn merge(\n        &self,\n        _shard_responses: Vec<ShardResponse>,\n        _offset: usize,\n        _limit: usize,\n        _client_requested_score: bool,\n    ) -> Result<MergedResult> {\n        Ok(MergedResult {\n            hits: Vec::new(),\n            facets: serde_json::json!({}),\n            total_hits: 0,\n            processing_time_ms: 0,\n            degraded: false,\n        })\n    }\n}\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use super::*;\n\n    fn create_hit(score: f64, id: &str) -> Value {\n        serde_json::json!({\n            \"id\": id,\n            \"_rankingScore\": score,\n            \"_miroir_shard\": 0,\n        })\n    }\n\n    fn create_shard_response(shard_id: u32, hits: Vec<Value>, total: u64) -> ShardResponse {\n        ShardResponse {\n            shard_id,\n            body: serde_json::json!({\n                \"hits\": hits,\n                \"estimatedTotalHits\": total,\n                \"processingTimeMs\": 10,\n                \"facetDistribution\": {},\n            }),\n            success: true,\n        }\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_global_sort_by_ranking_score() {\n        let merger = MergerImpl;\n\n        let hits1 = vec![\n            create_hit(0.5, \"doc1\"),\n            create_hit(0.9, \"doc2\"),\n            create_hit(0.3, \"doc3\"),\n        ];\n        let hits2 = vec![\n            create_hit(0.7, \"doc4\"),\n            create_hit(0.1, \"doc5\"),\n            create_hit(0.8, \"doc6\"),\n        ];\n\n        let shards = vec![\n            create_shard_response(0, hits1, 3),\n            create_shard_response(1, hits2, 3),\n        ];\n\n        let result = merger.merge(shards, 0, 10, false).unwrap();\n\n        // Check global ordering: should be doc2(0.9), doc6(0.8), doc4(0.7), doc1(0.5), doc3(0.3), doc5(0.1)\n        assert_eq!(result.hits.len(), 6);\n        assert_eq!(result.hits[0][\"id\"], \"doc2\");\n        assert_eq!(result.hits[1][\"id\"], \"doc6\");\n        assert_eq!(result.hits[2][\"id\"], \"doc4\");\n        assert_eq!(result.hits[3][\"id\"], \"doc1\");\n        assert_eq!(result.hits[4][\"id\"], \"doc3\");\n        assert_eq!(result.hits[5][\"id\"], \"doc5\");\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_offset_and_limit_applied_after_merge() {\n        let merger = MergerImpl;\n\n        let hits = vec![\n            create_hit(0.9, \"doc1\"),\n            create_hit(0.8, \"doc2\"),\n            create_hit(0.7, \"doc3\"),\n            create_hit(0.6, \"doc4\"),\n            create_hit(0.5, \"doc5\"),\n        ];\n\n        let shards = vec![create_shard_response(0, hits, 5)];\n\n        let result = merger.merge(shards, 2, 2, false).unwrap();\n\n        // Should skip first 2, take next 2\n        assert_eq!(result.hits.len(), 2);\n        assert_eq!(result.hits[0][\"id\"], \"doc3\");\n        assert_eq!(result.hits[1][\"id\"], \"doc4\");\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_ranking_score_stripped_when_not_requested() {\n        let merger = MergerImpl;\n\n        let hits = vec![create_hit(0.9, \"doc1\"), create_hit(0.8, \"doc2\")];\n\n        let shards = vec![create_shard_response(0, hits, 2)];\n\n        let result = merger.merge(shards, 0, 10, false).unwrap();\n\n        // _rankingScore should be stripped\n        assert!(result.hits[0].get(\"_rankingScore\").is_none());\n        assert!(result.hits[1].get(\"_rankingScore\").is_none());\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_ranking_score_included_when_requested() {\n        let merger = MergerImpl;\n\n        let hits = vec![create_hit(0.9, \"doc1\"), create_hit(0.8, \"doc2\")];\n\n        let shards = vec![create_shard_response(0, hits, 2)];\n\n        let result = merger.merge(shards, 0, 10, true).unwrap();\n\n        // _rankingScore should be present\n        assert_eq!(result.hits[0][\"_rankingScore\"], 0.9);\n        assert_eq!(result.hits[1][\"_rankingScore\"], 0.8);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_miroir_shard_always_stripped() {\n        let merger = MergerImpl;\n\n        let hits = vec![create_hit(0.9, \"doc1\")];\n\n        let shards = vec![create_shard_response(0, hits, 1)];\n\n        let result = merger.merge(shards, 0, 10, true).unwrap();\n\n        // _miroir_shard should always be stripped\n        assert!(result.hits[0].get(\"_miroir_shard\").is_none());\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_facet_counts_summed_across_shards() {\n        let merger = MergerImpl;\n\n        let shard1 = serde_json::json!({\n            \"hits\": [],\n            \"estimatedTotalHits\": 0,\n            \"processingTimeMs\": 10,\n            \"facetDistribution\": {\n                \"color\": {\n                    \"red\": 10,\n                    \"blue\": 5,\n                },\n                \"size\": {\n                    \"large\": 8,\n                }\n            }\n        });\n\n        let shard2 = serde_json::json!({\n            \"hits\": [],\n            \"estimatedTotalHits\": 0,\n            \"processingTimeMs\": 15,\n            \"facetDistribution\": {\n                \"color\": {\n                    \"red\": 7,\n                    \"green\": 3,\n                },\n                \"size\": {\n                    \"large\": 4,\n                    \"small\": 6,\n                }\n            }\n        });\n\n        let shards = vec![\n            ShardResponse {\n                shard_id: 0,\n                body: shard1,\n                success: true,\n            },\n            ShardResponse {\n                shard_id: 1,\n                body: shard2,\n                success: true,\n            },\n        ];\n\n        let result = merger.merge(shards, 0, 10, false).unwrap();\n\n        let facets = result.facets;\n        let color = facets.get(\"color\").unwrap().as_object().unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(color.get(\"red\").unwrap().as_u64().unwrap(), 17); // 10 + 7\n        assert_eq!(color.get(\"blue\").unwrap().as_u64().unwrap(), 5); // only in shard1\n        assert_eq!(color.get(\"green\").unwrap().as_u64().unwrap(), 3); // only in shard2\n\n        let size = facets.get(\"size\").unwrap().as_object().unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(size.get(\"large\").unwrap().as_u64().unwrap(), 12); // 8 + 4\n        assert_eq!(size.get(\"small\").unwrap().as_u64().unwrap(), 6); // only in shard2\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_estimated_total_hits_summed() {\n        let merger = MergerImpl;\n\n        let shards = vec![\n            create_shard_response(0, vec![], 100),\n            create_shard_response(1, vec![], 150),\n            create_shard_response(2, vec![], 75),\n        ];\n\n        let result = merger.merge(shards, 0, 10, false).unwrap();\n\n        assert_eq!(result.total_hits, 325); // 100 + 150 + 75\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_processing_time_max_across_shards() {\n        let merger = MergerImpl;\n\n        let shard1 = serde_json::json!({\n            \"hits\": [],\n            \"estimatedTotalHits\": 0,\n            \"processingTimeMs\": 10,\n        });\n\n        let shard2 = serde_json::json!({\n            \"hits\": [],\n            \"estimatedTotalHits\": 0,\n            \"processingTimeMs\": 25,\n        });\n\n        let shard3 = serde_json::json!({\n            \"hits\": [],\n            \"estimatedTotalHits\": 0,\n            \"processingTimeMs\": 15,\n        });\n\n        let shards = vec![\n            ShardResponse {\n                shard_id: 0,\n                body: shard1,\n                success: true,\n            },\n            ShardResponse {\n                shard_id: 1,\n                body: shard2,\n                success: true,\n            },\n            ShardResponse {\n                shard_id: 2,\n                body: shard3,\n                success: true,\n            },\n        ];\n\n        let result = merger.merge(shards, 0, 10, false).unwrap();\n\n        assert_eq!(result.processing_time_ms, 25); // max(10, 25, 15)\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_degraded_flag_when_shard_fails() {\n        let merger = MergerImpl;\n\n        let hits = vec![create_hit(0.9, \"doc1\")];\n\n        let shards = vec![\n            create_shard_response(0, hits.clone(), 1),\n            ShardResponse {\n                shard_id: 1,\n                body: serde_json::json!({}),\n                success: &NodeId,\n        method: &str,\n        path: &str,\n        body: Option<&[u8]>,\n        headers: &[(String, String)],\n    ) -> Result<NodeResponse> {\n        let node = topology\n            .node(node_id)\n            .ok_or_else(|| MiroirError::Routing(format!(\"node {} not found\", node_id.as_str())))?;\n\n        let url = format!(\"{}{}\", node.url, path);\n\n        let mut request self.client.patch(&url),\n            \"DELETE\" => self.client.delete(&url),\n            _ => {\n                return Err(MiroirError::Routing(format!(\n                    \"unsupported HTTP method: {method}\",\n                )))\n            }\n        };\n\n        // Add node master key header\n        request = request.header(\n            \"Authorization\",\n State(_state): State<ProxyState>,\n96\t    req: Request,\n97\t    next: Next,\n98\t) -> Response {\n99\t    let method = req.method().to_string();\n100\t    let path = req.uri().path().to_string();\n101\t\n102\t    REQUESTS_IN_FLIGHT.inc();\n103\t    let start = Instant::now();\n104\t\n105\t    let response = next.run(req).await;\n106\t\n107\t    let duration = REQUESTS_IN_FLIGHT.dec();\n120\t\n121\t    // Check for degraded header\n122\t    if response.headers().get(\"X-Miroir-Degraded\").is_some() {\n123\t        DEGRADED_REQUESTS_TOTAL\n124\t            .with_label_values(&[&method, &path])\n125\t            .inc();\n126\t    }\n127\t\n128\t    // Check for no quorum (503 status with specific error code)\n129\t    if status == 503 {\n130\t        DEGRADED_REQUESTS_TOTAL\n131\t            .with_label_values(&[&method, &path])\n132\t            .inc();\n133\t    }\n134\t\n135\t    response\n136\t}\n137\t\n138\t/// Export metrics in Prometheus Tracing/logging + Prometheus middleware\n\nuse axum::{\n    extract::{Request, State},\n    http::StatusCode,\n    middleware::Next,\n    response::Response,\n};\nuse crate::state::ProxyState;\nuse std::time::Instant;\nuse prometheus::{Counter, Histogram, IntGauge, Registry, TextEncoder};\nuse once_cell::sync::Lazy;\n\n/// Prometheus metrics registry.\n#[derive(Clone)]\npub struct Metrics {\n    pub registry: Registry,\n}\n\nimpl Metrics {\n    pub fn new() -> Self {\n        let registry = Registry::new();\n\n        // Register all metrics\n        registry.register(Box::new(REQUESTS_TOTAL.clone())).unwrap();\n        registry.register(Box::new(REQUEST_DURATION_SECONDS.clone())).unwrap();\n        registry.register(Box::new(REQUESTS_IN_FLIGHT.clone())).unwrap();\n        registry.register(Box::new(DEGRADED_REQUESTS_TOTAL.clone())).unwrap();\n        registry.register(Box::new(NO_QUORUM_REQUESTS_TOTAL.clone())).unwrap();\n\n        Self { registry }\n    }\n}\n\nimpl Default for Metrics {\n    fn default() -> Self {\n        Self::new()\n    }\n}\n\n/// Total number of requests.\nstatic REQUESTS_TOTAL: Lazy<Counter> = Lazy::new(|| {\n    Counter::new(\"miroir_requests_total\", \"Total number of requests\").unwrap()\n});\n\n/// Request duration in seconds.\nstatic REQUEST_DURATION_SECONDS: Lazy<Histogram> = Lazy::new(|| {\n    Histogram::with_opts(prometheus::HistogramOpts {\n        common_name: \"miroir_request_duration_seconds\".to_string(),\n        help: \"Request duration in seconds\".to_string(),\n        buckets: vec![0.005, 0.01, 0.025, 0.05, 0.1, 0.25, 0.5, 1.0, 2.5, 5.0, 10.0],\n    })\n    .unwrap()\n});\n\n/// Current number of requests in flight.\nstatic REQUESTS_IN_FLIGHT: Lazy<IntGauge> = Lazy::new(|| {\n    IntGauge::new(\"miroir_requests_in_flight\", \"Current number of requests in flight\").unwrap()\n});\n\n/// Total number of degraded requests.\nstatic DEGRADED_REQUESTS_TOTAL: Lazy<Counter> = Lazy::new(|| {\n    Counter::new(\"miroir_degraded_requests_total\", \"Total number of degraded requests\").unwrap()\n});\n\n/// Total number of requests that failed with no quorum.\nstatic NO_QUORUM_REQUESTS_TOTAL: Lazy<Counter> = Lazy::new(|| {\n    Counter::new(\"miroir_no_quorum_requests_total\", \"Total number of requests that failed with no quorum\").unwrap()\n});\n\n/// Tracing middleware that logs each request.\npub async fn tracing_middleware(req: Request, next: Next) -> Response {\n    let method = req.method().clone();\n    let uri = req.uri().clone();\n    let start = Instant::now();\n\n    let response = next.run(req).await;\n\n    let duration = start.elapsed();\n    let status = response.status();\n\n    tracing::info!(\n        method = %method,\n        uri = %uri,\n        status = status.as_u16(),\n        duration_ms = duration.as_millis(),\n        \"request completed\"\n    );\n\n    response\n}\n\n/// Prometheus metrics middleware.\npub async fn prometheus_middleware(\n    State(_state): State<ProxyState>,\n    req: Request,\n    next: Next,\n) -> Response {\n    let method = req.method().to_string();\n    let path = req.uri().path().to_string();\n\n    REQUESTS_IN_FLIGHT.inc();\n    let start = Instant::now();\n\n    let response = next.run(req).await;\n\n    let duration = start.elapsed().as_secs_f64();\n    let status = response.status().as_u16();\n\n    // Record metrics\n    REQUESTS_TOTAL\n        .with_label_values(&[&method, &path, &status.to_string()])\n        .inc();\n\n    REQUEST_DURATION_SECONDS\n        .with_label_values(&[&method, &path])\n        .observe(duration);\n\n    REQUESTS_IN_FLIGHT.dec();\n\n    // Check for degraded header\n    if response.headers().get(\"X-Miroir-Degraded\").is_some() {\n        DEGRADED_REQUESTS_TOTAL\n            .with_label_values(&[&method, &path])\n            .inc();\n    }\n\n    // Check for no quorum (503 status with specific error code)\n    if status == 503 {\n        DEGRADED_REQUESTS_TOTAL\n            .with_label_values(&[&method, &path])\n            .inc();\n    }\n\n    response\n}\n\n/// Export metrics in Prometheus text format.\npub fn export_metrics(metrics: &Metrics) -> String {\n    let encoder = TextEncoder::new();\n    let metric_families = metrics.registry.gather();\n    let mut buffer = Vec::new();\n\n    encoder.encode(&metric_families, &mut buffer).unwrap();\n\n    String::from_utf8(buffer).unwrap_or_else(|_| \"# Failed to encode metrics\\n\".to_string())\n}\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use super::*;\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_metrics_creation() {\n        let metrics = Metrics::new();\n        assert!(!metrics.registry.gather().is_empty());\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_export_metrics() {\n        let metrics = Metrics::new();\n        let output = export_metrics(&metrics);\n        assert!(output.contains(\"miroir_requests_total\"));\n        assert!(output.contains(\"miroir_request_duration_seconds\"));\n        assert!(output.contains(\"miroir_requests_in_flight\"));\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_metrics_default() {\n        let metrics = Metrics::default();\n        assert!(!metrics.registry.gather().is_empty());\n    }\n}\n","numLines":174,"startLine":1,"totalLines":174}}}

(/admin/*, /_miroir/*)\n//! - Rule 4: No bearer token → public endpoints only (/health, /version)\n//! - Rule 5: Invalid token → 403 Forbidden\n\nuse axum::{\n    extract::State,\n    http::{HeaderMap, StatusCode},\n    middleware::Next,\n    response::Response,\n};\nuse crate::state::ProxyState;\nuse crate::error_response::ErrorResponse;\n\n/// Token kind determined from the bearer token.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq)]\npub enum TokenKind {\n    /// Master key - full access to all endpoints.\n    Master,\n    /// Admin key - access to admin endpoints only.\n    Admin,\n}\n\n/// Authentication result from bearer token validation.\n#[derive(Debug)]\npub enum AuthResult {\n    /// Valid token with its kind.\n    Valid(TokenKind),\n    /// No bearer token present.\n    None,\n    /// Invalid bearer token.\n    Invalid,\n}\n\n/// Validate bearer token against the configured keys.\npub fn validate_bearer_token(headers: &HeaderMap, state: &ProxyState) -> AuthResult {\n    let auth_header = match headers.get(\"authorization\") {\n        Some(h) => h,\n        None => return AuthResult::None,\n    };\n\n    let auth_str = match auth_header.to_str() {\n        Ok(s) => s,\n        Err(_) => return AuthResult::Invalid,\n    };\n\n    let token = match auth_str.strip_prefix(\"Bearer \") {\n        Some(t) => t,\n        None => return AuthResult::None,\n    };\n\n    // Check master key first (rule 2)\n    if state.is_valid_master_key(token) {\n        return AuthResult::Valid(TokenKind::Master);\n    }\n\n    // Check admin key (rule 3)\n    if state.is_valid_admin_key(token) {\n        return AuthResult::Valid(TokenKind::Admin);\n    }\n\n    // Invalid token (rule 5)\n    AuthResult::Invalid\n}\n\n/// Check if a path requires authentication.\npub fn requires_auth(path: &str) -> bool {\n    // Public endpoints (rule 4)\n    if path == \"/health\" || path == \"/version\" {\n        return false;\n    }\n\n    true\n}\n\n/// Check if a path requires admin access.\npub fn requires_admin(path: &str) -> bool {\n    // Admin endpoints (rule 3)\n    if path.starts_with(\"/admin/\") || path.starts_with(\"/_miroir/\") {\n        return true;\n    }\n\n    // /metrics endpoint requires admin\n    if path == \"/metrics\" {\n        return true;\n    }\n\n    false\n}\n\n/// Authentication middleware.\n///\n/// Enforces bearer token validation per plan §5 rules 2-5.\npub async fn auth_middleware(\n    State(state): State<ProxyState>,\n    req: axum::extract::Request,\n    next: Next,\n) -> Result<Response, ErrorResponse> {\n    let path = req.uri().path();\n\n    // Check if authentication is required\n    if !requires_auth(path) {\n        return Ok(next.run(req).await);\n    }\n\n    // Validate bearer token\n    let auth_result = validate_bearer_token(req.headers(), &state);\n\n    match auth_result {\n        AuthResult::Valid(TokenKind::Master) => {\n            // Master key has full access (rule 2)\n            Ok(next.run(req).await)\n        }\n        AuthResult::Valid(TokenKind::Admin) => {\n            // Admin key can only access admin endpoints (rule 3)\n            if requires_admin(path) {\n                Ok(next.run(req).await)\n            } else {\n                Err(ErrorResponse::new(\n                    \"Admin key cannot access this endpoint. Use master key.\",\n                    \"invalid_api_key\",\n                ))\n            }\n        }\n        AuthResult::None => {\n            // No bearer token → 401 (rule 4)\n            Err(ErrorResponse::new(\n                \"Missing Authorization header\",\n                \"missing_authorization_header\",\n            ))\n        }\n        AuthResult::Invalid => {\n            // Invalid token → 403 (rule 5)\n            Err(ErrorResponse::new(\n                \"Invalid API key\",\n                \"invalid_api_key\",\n            ))\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use super::*;\n    use miroir_core::config::MiroirConfig;\n    use miroir_core::topology::{Node, NodeId};\n\n    fn test_state() -> ProxyState {\n        let mut  /// Group identifier (0-based).\n93\t    pub id: u32,\n94\t\n95\t    /// Nodes in this group.\n96\t    nodes: Vec<NodeId>,\n97\t}\n98\t\n99\timpl Group {\n100\t    /// Create a new group.\n101\t self.groups.push(Group::new(self.groups.len() as u32));\n156\t        }\n157\t\n158\t        self.groups[group_id].add_node(node.id.clone());\n159\t        self.nodes.insert(node.id.clone(), Topology management: node registry, groups, and health state.\n\nuse serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};\nuse std::collections::HashMap;\n\n/// Unique identifier for a node.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Hash, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct NodeId(String);\n\nimpl NodeId {\n    /// Create a new NodeId.\n    pub fn new(id: String) -> Self {\n        Self(id)\n    }\n\n    /// Get the node ID as a string slice.\n    pub fn as_str(&self) -> &str {\n        &self.0\n    }\n}\n\nimpl From<String> for NodeId {\n    fn from(s: String) -> Self {\n        Self(s)\n    }\n}\n\nimpl AsRef<str> for NodeId {\n    fn as_ref(&self) -> &str {\n        &self.0\n    }\n}\n\n/// Health status of a node.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub enum NodeStatus {\n    /// Node is healthy and serving traffic.\n    Healthy,\n    /// Node is degraded (intermittent failures, still serving traffic).\n    Degraded,\n    /// Node is active and fully operational (synonym for Healthy).\n    Active,\n    /// Node is joining the cluster (being provisioned).\n    Joining,\n    /// Node is draining (graceful shutdown, not accepting new writes).\n    Draining,\n    /// Node has failed (unplanned outage).\n    Failed,\n    /// Node has been removed from the cluster (tracked for migration).\n    Removed,\n}\n\n/// A single Meilisearch node in the topology.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct Node {\n    /// Unique node identifier.\n    pub id: NodeId,\n\n    /// Node base URL.\n    pub url: String,\n\n    /// Current health status.\n    pub status: NodeStatus,\n\n    /// Replica group assignment (0-based).\n    pub replica_group: u32,\n}\n\nimpl Node {\n    /// Create a new node.\n    pub fn new(id: NodeId, url: String, replica_group: u32) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            id,\n            url,\n            status: NodeStatus::Joining,\n            replica_group,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Check if the node is healthy (can serve traffic).\n    pub fn is_healthy(&self) -> bool {\n        matches!(self.status, NodeStatus::Healthy | NodeStatus::Active)\n    }\n}\n\n/// A replica group: an independent query pool.\n///\n/// Each group holds all S shards, distributed across its nodes.\n/// Reads are routed to a single group per query.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct Group {\n    /// Group identifier (0-based).\n    pub id: u32,\n\n    /// Nodes in this group.\n    nodes: Vec<NodeId>,\n}\n\nimpl Group {\n    /// Create a new group.\n    pub fn new(id: u32) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            id,\n            nodes: Vec::new(),\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Add a node to this group.\n    pub fn add_node(&mut self, node_id: NodeId) {\n        if !self.nodes.contains(&node_id) {\n            self.nodes.push(node_id);\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Get the nodes in this group.\n    pub fn nodes(&self) -> &[NodeId] {\n        &self.nodes\n    }\n\n    /// Get the number of nodes in this group.\n    pub fn node_count(&self) -> usize {\n        self.nodes.len()\n    }\n}\n\n/// Cluster topology: groups, nodes, and health state.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct Topology {\n    /// All nodes in the cluster.\n    nodes: HashMap<NodeId, Node>,\n\n    /// Replica groups.\n    groups: Vec<Group>,\n\n    /// Replication factor (intra-group).\n    rf: usize,\n}\n\nimpl Topology {\n    /// Create a new empty topology.\n    pub fn new(rf: usize) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            nodes: HashMap::new(),\n            groups: Vec::new(),\n            rf,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Add a node to the topology.\n    pub fn add_node(&mut self, node: Node) {\n        let group_id = node.replica_group as usize;\n\n        // Ensure group exists\n        while self.groups.len() <= group_id {\n            self.groups.push(Group::new(self.groups.len() as u32));\n        }\n\n        self.groups[group_id].add_node(node.id.clone());\n        self.nodes.insert(node.id.clone(), node);\n    }\n\n    /// Get a node by ID.\n    pub fn node(&self, id: &NodeId) -> Option<&Node> {\n        self.nodes.get(id)\n    }\n\n    /// Get all nodes.\n    pub fn nodes(&self) -> impl Iterator<Item = &Node> {\n        self.nodes.values()\n    }\n\n    /// Get a group by ID.\n    pub fn group(&self, id: u32) -> Option<&Group> {\n        self.groups.get(id as usize)\n    }\n\n    /// Iterate over all groups.\n    pub fn groups(&self) -> impl Iterator<Item = &Group> {\n        self.groups.iter()\n    }\n\n    /// Get the replication factor.\n    pub fn rf(&self) -> usize {\n        self.rf\n    }\n\n    /// Get the number of replica groups.\n    pub fn replica_group_count(&self) -> u32 {\n        self.groups.len() as u32\n    }\n}\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use super::*;\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_node_is_healthy() {\n        let mut node = Node::new(\n            NodeId::new(\"node1\".to_string()),\n            \"http://example.com\".to_string(),\n            0,\n        );\n\n        // Joining status is not healthy\n        assert!(!node.is_healthy());\n\n        // Healthy status is healthy\n        node.status = NodeStatus::Healthy;\n        assert!(node.is_healthy());\n\n        // Active status is healthy (synonym for Healthy)\n        node.status = NodeStatus::Active;\n        assert!(node.is_healthy());\n\n        // Degraded status is not healthy (intermittent failures)\n        node.status = NodeStatus::Degraded;\n        assert!(!node.is_healthy());\n\n        // Draining status is not healthy\n        node.status = NodeStatus::Draining;\n        assert!(!node.is_healthy());\n\n        // Failed status is not healthy\n        node.status = NodeStatus::Failed;\n        assert!(!node.is_healthy());\n\n        // Removed status is not healthy\n        node.status = NodeStatus::Removed;\n        assert!(!node.is_healthy());\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_group_node_count() {\n        let mut group = Group::new(0);\n        assert_eq!(group.node_count(), 0);\n\n        group.add_node(NodeId::new(\"node1\".to_string()));\n        assert_eq!(group.node_count(), 1);\n\n        group.add_node(NodeId::new(\"node2\".to_string()));\n        assert_eq!(group.node_count(), 2);\n\n        // Adding duplicate node doesn't increase count\n        group.add_node(NodeId::new(\"node1\".to_string()));\n        assert_eq!(group.node_count(), 2);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_topology_replica_group_count() {\n        let mut topology = Topology::new(2);\n\n        // Empty topology has 0 groups\n        assert_eq!(topology.replica_group_count(), 0);\n\n        // Add nodes to group 0\n        topology.add_node(Node::new(\n            NodeId::new(\"node1\".to_string()),\n            \"http://example.com\".to_string(),\n            0,\n        ));\n        assert_eq!(topology.replica_group_count(), 1);\n\n        // Add nodes to group 1\n        topology.add_node(Node::new(\n            NodeId::new(\"node2\".to_string()),\n            \"http://example.com\".to_string(),\n            1,\n        ));\n        assert_eq!(topology.replica_group_count(), 2);\n\n        // Add more nodes to existing groups\n get_all_settings(\n77\t    State(state): State<ProxyState>,\n78\t    Path(index): Path<String>,\n79\t) -> Result<Json<Value>, ErrorResponse> {\n80\t    let topology = update_setting_with_rollback(\n114\t    state: &ProxyState,\n115\t    index: &str,\n116\t    setting_path: &str,\n117\t    value: &Value,\n118\t) -> Result<Response, ErrorResponse> {\n119\t    let topology = state.topology().await;\n120\t    let targets = write_targets(0, &topology);\n121\t\n122\t    if targets.is_empty() {\n123\t        return Err(ErrorResponse::internal_error(\"No nodes available\"));\n124\t    }\n125\t\n126\t    let body_bytes = serde_json::to_vec(value).unwrap_or_default();\n127\t\n128\t    let request = ScatterRequest {\n129\t        method: \"PUT\".to_string(),\n130\t        path: format!(\"/indexes/{}/{}\", index, setting_path),\n131\t        body: body_bytes,\n132\t        headers: vec![],\n133\t    };\n134\t\n135\t    let scatter = HttpScatter::new((*state.client).clone(), state.config.server.request_timeout_ms);\n136\t\n137\t    // Track successful nodes for rollback\n138\t    let mut successful_nodes: Vec<String> = Vec::new();\n139\t    let mut last_response: Option<Value> = None;\n140\t\n141\t    // Sequential broadcast with rollback on failure\n142\t    for target in &targets {\n143\t        let result = scatter\n144\t            .scatter(&topology, vec![target.clone()], request.clone(), UnavailableShardPolicy::Error)\n145\t            .await;\n146\t\n147\t        match result {\n148\t            Ok(resp) => {\n149\t                if let Some(r) = resp.responses.first() {\n150\t                    if (200..300).contains(&r.status) {\n151\t                        successful_nodes.push(target.as_str().to_string());\n152\t                        last_response = Some(r.body.clone());\n153\t                    } else {\n154\t                        // Rollback from successful nodes\n155\t                        rollback_setting(state, &topology, &successful_nodes, index, setting_path).await;\n156\t                        return Err(ErrorResponse::internal_error(format!(\n157\t                            \"Failed to update setting on node {}: status {}\",\n158\t                            target.as_str(),\n159\t                            r.status\n160\t                        )));\n161\t                    }\n162\t                }\n163\t            }\n164\t            Err(e) => {\n165\t                // Rollback from successful nodes\n166\t                rollback_setting(state, &topology, &successful_nodes, index, setting_path).await;\n167\t                return Err(ErrorResponse::internal_error(format!(\n168\t                    \"Failed to update setting on node {}: {}\",\n169\t                    target.as_str(),\n170\t                    e\n171\t                )));\n172\t            }\n173\t        }\n174\t    }\n175\t\n176\t    let response_body = if let Some(body) = last_response {\n177\t        body\n178\t    } else {\n179\t        serde_json::json!({\n180\t            \"taskUid\": 1,\n181\t            \"indexUid\": index,\n182\t            \"status\": \"enqueued\",\n183\t            \"type\": \"settingsUpdate\",\n184\t            \"enqueuedAt\": chrono::Utc::now().to_rfc3339(),\n185\t        })\n186\t    };\n187\t\n188\t    Ok((axum::http::StatusCode::ACCEPTED, Json(response_body)).into_response())\n189\t}\n190\t\n191\t/// Rollback a setting from nodes that were successfully updated.\n192\tasync fn rollback_setting(\n193\t    state: &ProxyState,\n194\t    topology: &miroir_core::topology::Topology,\n195\t    successful_nodes: &[String],\n196\t    index: &str,\n197\t    setting_path: &str,\n198\t) {\n199\t    // For rollback, we need to get the original value first\n200\t    // This is a simplified version - in production, we'd cache original values\n201\t    for node_id in successful_nodes {\n202\t        let _ = state\n203\t            .client\n204\t            .send_to_node(\n205\t                topology,\n206\t              return Err(ErrorResponse::index_not_found(index));\n529\t                }\n530\t            }\n531\t        }\n532\t    }\n533\t\n534\t    Err(ErrorResponse::index_not_found(index))\n535\t}\n536\t\n537\t/// Generic DELETE handler for resetting a setting to default.\n538\tasync fn delete_setting(\n539\t    state: &ProxyState,\n540\t    index: &str,\n541\t    setting_path: &str,\n542\t) -> Result<Response, ErrorResponse> {\n543\t    let topology = state.topology().await;\n544\t    let targets = write_targets(0, &topology);\n545\t\n546\t    if targets.is_empty() {\n547\t        return Err(ErrorResponse::internal_error(\"No nodes available\"));\n548\t    }\n549\t\n550\t    let request = ScatterRequest {\n551\t        method: \"DELETE\".to_string(),\n552\t        path: format!(\"/indexes/{}/{}\", index, setting_path),\n553\t        body: vec![],\n554\t        headers: vec![],\n555\t    };\n556\t\n557\t    let scatter = HttpScatter::new((*state.client).clone(), state.config.server.request_timeout_ms);\n558\t    let result = scatter\n559\t        .scatter(&topology, targets, request,       let status = axum::http::StatusCode::from_u16(resp.status).unwrap_or(axum::http::StatusCode::OK);\n565\t        return Ok((status, Json(resp.body.clone())).into_response());\n566\t    }\n567\t\n568\t    Ok((axum::http::StatusCode::ACCEPTED, Json(serde_json::json!({}))).into_response())\n569\t}\n570\t\n571\t#[cfg(test)]\n572\tmod tests {\n573\t    use super::*;\n574\t\n575\t Settings routes: GET, PATCH, DELETE /indexes/:index/settings\n//!\n//! Implements settings broadcast per plan §3:\n//! - Sequential apply-with-rollback on failure\n//! - Broadcast to all nodes\n//! - Rollback from successful nodes if any fail\n\nuse axum::{\n    extract::{Path, State},\n    response::{IntoResponse, Json, Response},\n};\nuse miroir_core::{\n    config::UnavailableShardPolicy,\n    router::write_targets,\n    scatter::{Scatter, ScatterRequest},\n};\nuse serde_json::Value;\n\nuse crate::{\n    error_response::ErrorResponse,\n    scatter::HttpScatter,\n    state::ProxyState,\n};\n\n/// Settings router.\npub fn router() -> axum::Router {\n    axum::Router::new()\n        .route(\"/\", axum::routing::get(get_all_settings))\n        .route(\n            \"/filterable-attributes\",\n            axum::routing::get(get_filterable_attributes).put(update_filterable_attributes).delete(delete_filterable_attributes),\n        )\n        .route(\n            \"/searchable-attributes\",\n            axum::routing::get(get_searchable_attributes).put(update_searchable_attributes).delete(delete_searchable_attributes),\n        )\n        .route(\n            \"/sortable-attributes\",\n            axum::routing::get(get_sortable_attributes).put(update_sortable_attributes).delete(delete_sortable_attributes),\n        )\n        .route(\n            \"/displayed-attributes\",\n            axum::routing::get(get_displayed_attributes).put(update_displayed_attributes).delete(delete_displayed_attributes),\n        )\n        .route(\n            \"/ranking-rules\",\n            axum::routing::get(get_ranking_rules).put(update_ranking_rules).delete(delete_ranking_rules),\n        )\n        .route(\n            \"/stop-words\",\n            axum::routing::get(get_stop_words).put(update_stop_words).delete(delete_stop_words),\n        )\n        .route(\n            \"/synonyms\",\n            axum::routing::get(get_synonyms).put(update_synonyms).delete(delete_synonyms),\n        )\n        .route(\n            \"/distinct-attribute\",\n            axum::routing::get(get_distinct_attribute).put(update_distinct_attribute).delete(delete_distinct_attribute),\n        )\n        .route(\n            \"/typo-tolerance\",\n            axum::routing::get(get_typo_tolerance).put(update_typo_tolerance).delete(delete_typo_tolerance),\n        )\n        .route(\n            \"/faceting\",\n            axum::routing::get(get_faceting).put(update_faceting).delete(delete_faceting),\n        )\n        .route(\n            \"/pagination\",\n            axum::routing::get(get_pagination).put(update_pagination).delete(delete_pagination),\n        )\n}\n\n/// GET /indexes/:index/settings - Get all settings.\nasync fn get_all_settings(\n    State(state): State<ProxyState>,\n    Path(index): Path<String>,\n) -> Result<Json<Value>, ErrorResponse> {\n    let topology = state.topology().await;\n\n    // Query first available node\n    for group in topology.groups() {\n        if let Some(node_id) = group.nodes().first() {\n            let request = ScatterRequest {\n                method: \"GET\".to_string(),\n                path: format!(\"/indexes/{}/settings\", index),\n                body: vec![],\n                headers: vec![],\n            };\n\n            let scatter = HttpScatter::new((*state.client).clone(), state.config.server.request_timeout_ms);\n\n            let result = scatter\n                .scatter(&topology, vec![node_id.clone()], request, UnavailableShardPolicy::Partial)\n                .await\n                .map_err(|e| ErrorResponse::internal_error(e.to_string()))?;\n\n            if let Some(resp) = result.responses.first() {\n                if resp.status == 200 {\n                    return Ok(Json(resp.body.clone()));\n                } else if resp.status == 404 {\n                    return Err(ErrorResponse::index_not_found(&index));\n                }\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    Err(ErrorResponse::index_not_found(&index))\n}\n\n/// Generic handler for updating a setting with rollback.\nasync fn update_setting_with_rollback(\n    state: &ProxyState,\n    index: &str,\n    setting_path: &str,\n    value: &Value,\n) -> Result<Response, ErrorResponse> {\n    let topology = state.topology().await;\n    let targets = write_targets(0, &topology);\n\n    if targets.is_empty() {\n        return Err(ErrorResponse::internal_error(\"No nodes available\"));\n    }\n\n    let body_bytes = serde_json::to_vec(value).unwrap_or_default();\n\n    let request = ScatterRequest {\n        method: \"PUT\".to_string(),\n        path: format!(\"/indexes/{}/{}\", index, setting_path),\n        body: body_bytes,\n        headers: vec![],\n    };\n\n    let scatter = HttpScatter::new((*state.client).clone(), state.config.server.request_timeout_ms);\n\n    // Track successful nodes for rollback\n    let mut successful_nodes: Vec<String> = Vec::new();\n    let mut last_response: Option<Value> = None;\n\n    // Sequential broadcast with rollback on failure\n    for target in &targets {\n        let result = scatter\n            .scatter(&topology, vec![target.clone()], request.clone(), UnavailableShardPolicy::Error)\n            .await;\n\n        match result {\n            Ok(resp) => {\n                if let Some(r) = resp.responses.first() {\n                    if (200..300).contains(&r.status) {\n                        successful_nodes.push(target.as_str().to_string());\n                        last_response = Some(r.body.clone());\n                    } else {\n                        // Rollback from successful nodes\n                        rollback_setting(state, &topology, &successful_nodes, index, setting_path).await;\n                        return Err(ErrorResponse::internal_error(format!(\n                            \"Failed to update setting on node {}: status {}\",\n                            target.as_str(),\n                            r.status\n                        )));\n                    }\n                }\n            }\n            Err(e) => {\n                // Rollback from successful nodes\n                rollback_setting(state, &topology, &successful_nodes, index, setting_path).await;\n                return Err(ErrorResponse::internal_error(format!(\n                    \"Failed to update setting on node {}: {}\",\n                    target.as_str(),\n                    e\n                )));\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    let response_body = if let Some(body) = last_response {\n        body\n    } else {\n        serde_json::json!({\n            \"taskUid\": 1,\n            \"indexUid\": index,\n            \"status\": \"enqueued\",\n            \"type\": \"settingsUpdate\",\n            \"enqueuedAt\": chrono::Utc::now().to_rfc3339(),\n        })\n    };\n\n    Ok((axum::http::StatusCode::ACCEPTED, Json(response_body)).into_response())\n}\n\n/// Rollback a setting from nodes that were successfully updated.\nasync fn rollback_setting(\n    state: &ProxyState,\n    topology: &miroir_core::topology::Topology,\n    successful_nodes: &[String],\n    index: &str,\n    setting_path: &str,\n) {\n    // For rollback, we need to get the original value first\n    // This is a simplified version - in production, we'd cache original values\n    for node_id in successful_nodes {\n        let _ = state\n            .client\n            .send_to_node(\n                topology,\n                &node_id.as_str().into(),\n                \"DELETE\",\n                &format!(\"/indexes/{}/{}\", index, setting_path),\n                None,\n                &[],\n            )\n            .await;\n    }\n}\n\n/// GET /indexes/:index/settings/filterable-attributes\nasync fn get_filterable_attributes(\n    State(state): State<ProxyState>,\n    Path(index): Path<String>,\n) -> Result<Json<Value>, ErrorResponse> {\n ErrorResponse> {\n    let topology = state.topology().await;\n\n    for group in topology.groups() {\n        if let Some(node_id) = group.nodes().first() {\n            let request = ScatterRequest {\n                method: \"GET\".to_string(),\n                path: format!(\"/indexes/{}/{}\", index, setting_path),\n                body: vec![],\n                headers: vec![],\n            };\n\n            let scatter = HttpScatter::new((*state.client).clone(), state.config.server.request_timeout_ms);\n\n            let result = scatter\n                .scatter(&topology, vec![node_id.clone()], request, UnavailableShardPolicy::Partial)\n                .await\n                .map_err(|e| ErrorResponse::internal_error(e.to_string()))?;\n\n            if let Some(resp) = result.responses.first() {\n                if resp.status == 200 {\n                    return Ok(Json(resp.body.clone()));\n                } else if resp.status == 404 {\n                    return Err(ErrorResponse::index_not_found(index));\n                }\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    Err(ErrorResponse::index_not_found(index))\n}\n\n/// Generic DELETE handler for resetting a setting to default.\nasync fn delete_setting(\n    state: &ProxyState,\n    index: &str,\n    setting_path: &str,\n) -> Result<Response, ErrorResponse> {\n    let topology = state.topology().await;\n    let targets = write_targets(0, &topology);\n\n    if targets.is_empty() {\n        return Err(ErrorResponse::internal_error(\"No nodes available\"));\n    }\n\n    let request = ScatterRequest {\n        method: \"DELETE\".to_string(),\n        path: format!(\"/indexes/{}/{}\", index, setting_path),\n        body: vec![],\n        headers: vec![],\n    };\n\n    let scatter = HttpScatter::new((*state.client).clone(), state.config.server.request_timeout_ms);\n    let result = scatter\n        .scatter(&topology, targets, request, UnavailableShardPolicy::Partial)\n axum::http::StatusCode::from_u16(resp.status).unwrap_or(axum::http::StatusCode::OK);\n        return Ok((status, Json(resp.body.clone())).into_response());\n    }\n\n self.base_url, path))\n53\t            .send()\n54\t            .await\n55\t            .unwrap()\n56\t    }\n57\t}\n58\t\n59\tstruct TestCluster {\n60\t    proxy_url: String,\n61\t    nodes: .enumerate()\n69\t            .map(|(i, port)| TestNode::new(format!(\"node-{}\", i), port))\n70\t            .collect();\n71\t\n72\t        Self {\n73\t            proxy_url: .await\n89\t            .unwrap()\n90\t    }\n91\t\n92\t    async fn add_documents(&self, index: &str, documents: Vec<serde_json::Value>) -> reqwest::Response {\n93\t        let client =     .await\n99\t            .unwrap()\n100\t    }\n101\t\n102\t    async fn search(&self, index: &str, query: serde_json::Value) -> reqwest::Response {\n103\t        let client = format!(\"http://127.0.0.1:{}\", port),\n        }\n    }\n\n    async fn get(&self, path: &str) -> reqwest::Response {\n        let client = reqwest::Client::new();\n        client\n            .get(format!(\"{}{}\", self.base_url, path))\n            .send()\n            .await\n            .unwrap()\n    }\n\n    async fn post(&self, path: &str, body: serde_json::Value) -> reqwest::Response {\n        let client = reqwest::Client::new();\n        client\n            .post(format!(\"{}{}\", self.base_url, path))\n            .json(&body)\n            .send()\n            .await\n            .unwrap()\n    }\n\n    async fn delete(&self, path: &str) -> reqwest::Response {\n        let client = reqwest::Client::new();\n        client\n            .delete(format!(\"{}{}\", self.base_url, path))\n            .send()\n            .await\n            .unwrap()\n    }\n}\n\nstruct TestCluster {\n    proxy_url: String,\n    nodes: Vec<TestNode>,\n}\n\nimpl TestCluster {\n    fn new(proxy_port: u16, node_ports: Vec<u16>) -> Self {\n        let nodes = node_ports\n            .into_iter()\n            .enumerate()\n            .map(|(i, port)| TestNode::new(format!(\"node-{}\", i), port))\n            .collect();\n\n        Self {\n            proxy_url: format!(\"http://127.0.0.1:{}\", proxy_port),\n            nodes,\n        }\n    }\n\n    async fn create_index(&self, uid: &str, primary_key: Option<&str>) -> reqwest::Response {\n        let client = reqwest::Client::new();\n        let mut body = serde_json::json!({ \"uid\": uid });\n        if let Some(pk) = primary_key {\n            body[\"primaryKey\"] = serde_json::json!(pk);\n        }\n        client\n            .post(format!(\"{}/indexes\", self.proxy_url))\n            .json(&body)\n            .send()\n            .await\n            .unwrap()\n    }\n\n    async fn add_documents(&self, index: &str, documents: Vec<serde_json::Value>) -> reqwest::Response {\n        let client = reqwest::Client::new();\n        client\n            .post(format!(\"{}/indexes/{}/documents\", self.proxy_url, index))\n            .json(&documents)\n            .send()\n            .await\n            .unwrap()\n    }\n\n    async fn search(&self, index: &str, query: serde_json::Value) -> reqwest::Response {\n        let client = reqwest::Client::new();\n        client\n            .post(format!(\"{}/indexes/{}/search\", self.proxy_url, index))\n            .json(&query)\n            .send()\n            .await\n            .unwrap()\n    }\n\n    async fn get_document(&self, index: &str, id: &str) -> reqwest::Response {\n        let client = reqwest::Client::new();\n        client\n            .get(format!(\n retrievable by ID\n#[tokio::test]\n#[ignore] // Requires running nodes\nasync fn test_1000_documents_indexed_retrievable_by_id() {\n    let cluster = TestCluster::new(7700, vec![7701, 7702, 7703]);\n\n    // Create index\n    let create_resp = cluster.create_index(\"test_index\", Some(\"id\")).await;\n    assert!(create_resp.status().is_success());\n\n    // Wait for index creation\n    tokio::time::sleep(tokio::time::Duration::from_millis(500)).await;\n\n    // Create 1000 documents\n    let documents: Vec<serde_json::Value> = (0..1000)\n        .map(|i| {\n .collect();\n\n    // Add documents in batches\n    for chunk in documents.chunks(100) {\n        let resp = cluster.add_documents(\"test_index\", chunk.to_vec()).await;\n        assert!(resp.status().is_success());\n    }\n\n    // Wait for indexing\n    tokio::time::sleep(tokio::time::Duration::from_secs(2)).await;\n\n    // Verify each document is retrievable by ID\n    for assert_eq!(doc[\"id\"], id);\n        assert_eq!(doc[\"value\"], i);\n    }\n}\n\n/// Test: Unique-keyword search finds every doc exactly once\n#[tokio::test]\n#[ignore]\nasync fn test_unique_keyword_search_finds_all_docs_once() {\n    let cluster = TestCluster::new(7700, vec![7701, 7702, 7703]);\n\n    // Create index\n    let create_resp = cluster.create_index(\"search_test\", Some(\"id\")).await;\n    assert!(create_resp.status().is_success());\n\n    tokio::time::sleep(tokio::time::Duration::from_millis(500)).await;\n\n    // Create documents with unique keywords\n    let documents: Vec<serde_json::Value> = (0..100)\n        .map(|i| {\n            serde_json::json!({\n                \"id\": format!(\"unique-doc-{}\", i),\n                \"keyword\": format!(\"unique-keyword-{}\", i),\n                \"value\": i,\n            })\n        })\n        .collect();\n\n    let resp = cluster.add_documents(\"search_test\", result\n    for i in 0..100 {\n        let keyword = format!(\"unique-keyword-{}\", i);\n        let search_resp = cluster\n            .search(\n                \"search_test\",\n                serde_json::json!({ \"q\": keyword, \"limit\": 100 }),\n            )\n            .await;\n\n        assert!(search_resp.status().is_success());\n\n        let results: serde_json::Value = search_resp.json().await.unwrap();\n        let hits = results[\"hits\"].as_array().unwrap();\n\n        assert_eq!(\n            hits.len(),\n            1,\n            \"Expected exactly 1 result for keyword {}, got {}\",\n            keyword,\n            hits.len()\n        );\n\n        assert_eq!(hits[0][\"keyword\"], keyword);\n        assert_eq!(hits[0][\"value\"], i);\n    }\n\n    // Search without query should return all docs\n    let all_resp = cluster\n        .search(\"search_test\", serde_json::json!({ \"q\": \"\", \"limit\": 200 }))\n        .await;\n\n    let all_results:       id\n        );\n    }\n\n    assert_eq!(seen_ids.len(), 100, \"Expected 100 unique documents\");\n}\n\n/// Test: Facet aggregation across 3 color values sums correctly\n#[tokio::test]\n#[ignore]\nasync fn test_facet_aggregation_sums_correctly() {\n    let cluster = TestCluster::new(7700, vec![7701, 7702, 7703]);\n\n    // Create index with filterable .await\n        .unwrap();\n    assert!(filter_resp.status().is_success());\n\n    tokio::time::sleep(tokio::time::Duration::from_millis(500)).await;\n\n    // Create documents with 3 color values \"value\": i,\n            })\n        })\n        .collect();\n\n    let resp = cluster.add_documents(\"facet_test\", documents).await;\n    assert!(resp.status().is_success());\n\n serde_json::json!({\n                \"q\": \"\",\n                \"facets\": [\"color\"],\n                \"limit\": 0\n            }),\n        )\n        .await;\n\n    assert!(search_resp.status().is_success());\n\n    let results: serde_json::Value = search_resp.json().await.unwrap();\n    let facet_dist = global ordering\n#[tokio::test]\n#[ignore]\nasync fn test_offset_limit_paging_preserves_global_ordering() {\n    let cluster = TestCluster::new(7700, vec![7701, 7702, 7703]);\n\n    // Create index\n    let create_resp = cluster.create_index(\"paging_test\", Some(\"id\")).await;\n    assert!(create_resp.status().is_success());\n\n    tokio::time::sleep(tokio::time::Duration::from_millis(500)).await;\n\n    // Create documents with sequential values\n    let documents: Vec<serde_json::Value> = (0..100)\n        .map(|i| {\n .collect();\n\n    let resp = cluster.add_documents(\"paging_test\", documents).await;\n    assert!(resp.status().is_success());\n\n    tokio::time::sleep(tokio::time::Duration::from_secs(1)).await;\n\n    // Fetch all documents in pages\n    let mut all_values: Vec<i64> = Vec::new();\n    let page_size = 10;\n\n    for page in 0..10      let value = hit[\"value\"].as_i64().unwrap();\n            all_values.push(value);\n        }\n    }\n\n    // Verify we got exactly 100 unique values\n    assert_eq!(all_values.len(), 100);\n\n    // Verify global ordering is preserved (no duplicates, all 0-99 present)\n    let mut seen = HashSet::new();\n    for value in all_values {\n        assert!(\n            seen.insert(value),\n .scatter(&topology, unique_nodes.into_iter().collect(), request, policy)\n87\t            .await?;\n88\t\n89\t        // Convert node responses to shard responses\n90\t        let mut shard_responses: Search read path: scatter-gather with result merging.\n\nuse crate::scatter::HttpScatter;\nuse crate::state::ProxyState;\nuse miroir_core::config::UnavailableShardPolicy;\nuse miroir_core::merger::{Merger, MergerImpl, ShardResponse};\nuse miroir_core::router;\nuse miroir_core::scatter::ScatterRequest;\nuse miroir_core::topology::{NodeId, Topology};\nuse miroir_core::{MiroirError, Result};\nuse serde_json::{json, Value};\nuse std::collections::HashMap;\n\n/// Search executor for scatter-gather queries.\npub struct SearchExecutor {\n    state: ProxyState,\n    scatter: HttpScatter,\n    merger: MergerImpl,\n}\n\nimpl SearchExecutor {\n    pub fn new(state: ProxyState) -> Self {\n        let node_timeout_ms = state.config.scatter.node_timeout_ms;\n        let scatter = HttpScatter::new(state.client.clone(), node_timeout_ms);\n\n        Self {\n            state,\n            scatter,\n            merger: MergerImpl,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Execute a search query across the covering set.\n    pub async fn search(\n        &self,\n        index: &str,\n        query: Value,\n        offset: usize,\n        limit: usize,\n    ) -> Result<SearchResult> {\n        let topology = self.state.topology().await;\n        let shard_count = self.state.config.shards;\n        let rf = self.state.config.replication_factor as usize;\n        let replica_groups = topology.replica_group_count();\n\n        // Select query group\n        let query_seq = self.state.next_query_seq();\n        let group_id = router::query_group(query_seq, replica_groups);\n\n        let group = topology\n            .group(group_id)\n            .ok_or_else(|| MiroirError::Routing(format!(\"Group {} not found\", group_id)))?;\n\n        // Build covering set\n        let covering = router::covering_set(shard_count, group, rf, query_seq);\n\n        // Deduplicate nodes\n        let unique_nodes: std::collections::HashSet<_> = covering.into_iter().collect();\n\n        // Prepare search query\n        let mut query_with_score = query.clone();\n        if let Some(obj) = query_with_score.as_object_mut() {\n            obj.insert(\"showRankingScore\".to_string(), json!(true));\n        }\n\n        let body = serde_json::to_vec(&query_with_score).unwrap();\n        let path = format!(\"/indexes/{}/search\", index);\n\n        let request = ScatterRequest {\n            body,\n            headers: vec![],\n            method: \"POST\".to_string(),\n            path,\n        };\n\n        // Get policy from config\n        let policy = match self.state.config.scatter.unavailable_shard_policy.as_str() {\n            \"error\" => UnavailableShardPolicy::Error,\n            \"fallback\" => UnavailableShardPolicy::Fallback,\n            _ => UnavailableShardPolicy::Partial,\n        };\n\n        // Scatter to covering set\n        let response = self\n            .scatter\n            .scatter(&topology, unique_nodes.into_iter().collect(), request, policy)\n            .await?;\n\n        // Convert node responses to shard responses\n        let mut shard_responses: Vec<ShardResponse> = Vec::new();\n        let mut degraded_shards = Vec::new();\n\n        for node_resp in response.responses {\n            // Parse response as shard response (all shards from this node)\n            shard_responses.push(ShardResponse {\n                shard_id: 0, // We'll merge all responses together\n                body: serde_json::from_slice(&node_resp.body).unwrap_or(json!({})),\n                success: true,\n            });\n        }\n\n        for failed_node in &response.failed {\n            degraded_shards.push(failed_node.as_str().to_string());\n        }\n\n        // Check if client requested ranking score\n        let client_requested_score = query\n            .get(\"showRankingScore\")\n            .and_then(|v| v.as_bool())\n            .unwrap_or(false);\n\n        // Merge results\n        let merged = self\n            .merger\n            .merge(shard_responses, offset, limit, client_requested_score)?;\n\n        // Build response\n        let mut result = json!({\n            \"hits\": merged.hits,\n            \"processingTimeMs\": merged.processing_time_ms,\n            \"query\": query,\n        });\n\n        if !merged.facets.is_null() {\n            if let Some(obj) = result.as_object_mut() {\n                obj.insert(\"facetDistribution\".to_string(), merged.facets);\n            }\n        }\n\n        // Add estimatedTotalHits if present\n        if merged.total_hits > 0 {\n            if let Some(obj) = result.as_object_mut() {\n                obj.insert(\"estimatedTotalHits\".to_string(), json!(merged.total_hits));\n            }\n        }\n\n        Ok(SearchResult {\n            body: result,\n            degraded: merged.degraded,\n            degraded_shards,\n        })\n    }\n}\n\n/// Result of a search operation.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\npub struct SearchResult {\n    pub body: Value,\n    pub degraded: bool,\n    pub degraded_shards: Vec<String>,\n}\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use super::*;\n    use miroir_core::config::MiroirConfig;\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn test_search_result_creation() {\n        let result = SearchResult {\n            body: json!({\"hits\": []}),\n            degraded: false,\n            degraded_shards: vec![],\n        };\n\n        assert_eq!(result.body[\"hits\"].as_array().unwrap().len(), 0);\n        assert!(!result.degraded);\n    }\n\n    fn create_test_executor() -> SearchExecutor {\n        let config = MiroirConfig {\n            shards: 64,\n            replication_factor: 2,\n            ..Default::default()\n        };\n\n        let state = ProxyState::new(config).unwrap();\n        SearchExecutor::new(state)\n    }\n}\n","numLines":181,"startLine":1,"totalLines":181}}}

    &topology,\n                    &node.id,\n                    \"GET\",\n                    &format!(\"/indexes/{}/settings/filterable-attributes\", uid),\n                    None,\n Write path: document routing with hash-based sharding and quorum.\n\nuse crate::client::NodeClient;\nuse crate::error_response::ErrorResponse;\nuse crate::scatter::HttpScatter;\nuse crate::state::ProxyState;\nuse miroir_core::config::UnavailableShardPolicy;\nuse miroir_core::router;\nuse miroir_core::scatter::ScatterRequest;\nuse miroir_core::topology::Topology;\nuse document batches.\npub struct WriteExecutor {\n    state: ProxyState,\n    scatter: HttpScatter,\n}\n\nimpl WriteExecutor {\n    pub fn new(state: ProxyState) -> Self {\n        let node_timeout_ms = state.config.scatter.node_timeout_ms;\n        let scatter = HttpScatter::new(state.client.clone(), node_timeout_ms);\n\n        Self { state, scatter }\n    }\n\n    /// Execute a document write (add/replace) for an index.\n    pub async fn write_documents(\n        &self,\n        index: &str,\n        documents: Vec<Value>,\n        primary_key: Option<&str>,\n    ) -> Result<WriteResult> {\n        // Validate primary key is known\n        let pk = self.resolve_primary_key(index, primary_key).await?;\n\n        // Hash documents by shard and group by target nodes\n        let topology = self.state.topology().await;\n        let shard_count = self.state.config.shards;\n        let rf = self.state.config.replication_factor as usize;\n\n        let mut shard_groups: HashMap<u32, Vec<Value>> = HashMap::new();\n        let mut reserved_field_errors = Vec::new();\n\n        for (idx, doc) in documents.iter().enumerate() {\n            // Check for reserved fields\n            if let Some(obj) = doc.as_object() {\n                if obj.contains_key(\"_miroir_shard\") {\n                    reserved_field_errors.push(idx);\n                    continue;\n                }\n            }\n\n            // Extract primary key value\n            let pk_value = self.extract_pk_value(doc, &pk)?;\n            let shard_id = router::shard_for_key(&pk_value, shard_count);\n\n            // Inject _miroir_shard\n            let mut doc_with_shard = doc.clone();\n            if let Some(obj) = doc_with_shard.as_object_mut() {\n                obj.insert(\"_miroir_shard\".to_string(), json!(shard_id));\n            }\n\n            shard_groups\n                .entry(shard_id)\n                .or_insert_with(Vec::new)\n                .push(doc_with_shard);\n        }\n\n        if !reserved_field_errors.is_empty() {\n            return Err(MiroirError::Routing(format!(\n                \"{} documents contain reserved field _miroir_shard: {:?}\",\n                reserved_field_errors.len(),\n                reserved_field_errors\n            )));\n        }\n\n        // For each shard, compute write targets and group by node\n        let mut node_batches: HashMap<String, Vec<Value>> = HashMap::new();\n\n        for (shard_id, docs) in shard_groups {\n            let targets = router::write_targets(shard_id, &topology);\n\n            for target in targets {\n                let node = topology\n                    .node(&target)\n                    .ok_or_else(|| MiroirError::Routing(format!(\"node {} not found\", target.as_str())))?;\n\n                node_batches\n                    .entry(node.id.as_str().to_string())\n                    .or_insert_with(Vec::new)\n                    .extend(docs.clone());\n            }\n        }\n\n        // Fan out writes to all nodes\n        let miroir_task_id = format!(\"mtask-{}\", Uuid::new_v4());\n\n        let mut node_tasks: HashMap<String, u64> = HashMap::new();\n        let mut group_quorum: HashMap<u32, GroupQuorum> = HashMap::new();\n        let mut failed_nodes = Vec::new();\n\n        for (node_id, docs) in node_batches {\n            let body = serde_json::to_vec(&docs).unwrap();\n            let path = format!(\"/indexes/{}/documents\", index);\n\n            let request = ScatterRequest {\n                body,\n                headers: vec![],\n                method: \"POST\".to_string(),\n                path,\n            };\n\n            // Send to this node\n            let result = self\n                .scatter\n                .scatter(&topology, vec![node_id.clone().into()], request, UnavailableShardPolicy::Partial)\n                .await?;\n\n            if let Some(resp) = result.responses.first() {\n                // Parse response to get task UID\n                if let Some(task_uid) = resp.body.get(\"taskUid\").and_then(|v| v.as_u64()) {\n group_id,\n                                rf,\n                                acked: HashSet::new(),\n                            }\n                        });\n                        quorum.acked.insert(node_id.clone());\n                    }\n                } else {\n                    failed_nodes.push(node_id);\n                }\n            } else {\n                failed_nodes.push(node_id);\n            }\n        }\n\n        // Check quorum - write succeeds if at least one group met quorum\n        let degraded_groups = self.check_quorum(&group_quorum, &topology);\n        let any_group_met_quorum = group_quorum.values().any(|q| q.met_quorum());\n\n        if !any_group_met_quorum {\n            return Err(MiroirError::Routing(\"No replica group met quorum\".to_string()));\n        }\n\n        Ok(WriteResult {\n            miroir_task_id,\n            node_tasks,\n            degraded_groups,\n        })\n    }\n\n    async fn resolve_primary_key(&self, index: &str, primary_key: Option<&str>) -> Result<String> {\n        if let Some(pk) = primary_key {\n            return Ok(pk.to_string());\n        }\n\n        // Query index to get primary key\n        let topology = self.state.topology().await;\n        let first_node = topology.nodes().next();\n\n        if let Some(node) = first_node {\n            let resp = self\n                .state\n                .client\n                .send_to_node(&topology, &node.id, \"GET\", &format!(\"/indexes/{}\", index), None, &[])\n                .await?;\n\n            if let Some(pk) = resp.body.get(\"primaryKey\").and_then(|v| v.as_str()) {\n                return Ok(pk.to_string());\n            }\n        }\n\n        Err(MiroirError::Routing(format!(\n            \"Index {} does not have a primary key\",\n            index\n        )))\n    }\n\n    fn extract_pk_value(&self, doc: &Value, pk: &str) -> Result<String> {\n        let obj = doc\n            .as_object()\n            .ok_or_else(|| MiroirError::Routing(\"Document is not an object\".to_string()))?;\n\n        let value = obj.get(pk).ok_or_else(|| {\n            MiroirError::Routing(format!(\"Primary key '{}' not found in document\", pk))\n        })?;\n\n        Ok(value.to_string())\n    }\n\n    fn check_quorum(&self, group_quorum: &HashMap<u32, GroupQuorum>, topology: &Topology) -> Vec<u32> {\n        let mut degraded = Vec::new();\n\n        for (group_id, quorum) in group_quorum {\n            if !quorum.met_quorum() {\n                degraded.push(*group_id);\n            }\n        }\n\n        degraded\n    }\n}\n\n/// Result of a document write operation.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\npub struct WriteResult {\n    pub miroir_task_id: String,\n    pub node_tasks: HashMap<String, u64>,\n    pub degraded_groups: Vec<u32>,\n}\n\n/// Quorum tracking for a replica group.\n#[derive(Debug)]\nstruct GroupQuorum {\n required\n    }\n}\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use super::*;\n    use miroir_core::config::{MiroirConfig, NodeConfig, ServerConfig};\n    use miroir_core::topology::{Node, NodeId, Topology};\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn test_extract_pk_value() {\n        let doc = json!({\"id\": \"test123\", \"name\": \"foo\"});\n        let executor = create_test_executor();\n\n        let result = executor.extract_pk_value(&doc, \"id\").unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(result, \"\\\"test123\\\"\");\n    }\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn test_extract_pk_value_missing() {\n        let doc = builder.add_source(ext_config::Config::try_from(&MiroirConfig::default())?);\n\n    for path in CONFIG_SEARCH_PATHS {\n        if std::path::Path::new(path).exists() {\n            builder = builder.add_source(ext_config::File::with_name(path));\n            break;\n        }\n    }\n\n    builder = builder.add_source(\n        ext_config::Environment::with_prefix(ENV_PREFIX)\n            .separator(\"_\")\n            .try_parsing(true),\n    );\n\n    let cfg: MiroirConfig = builder.build()?.try_deserialize()?;\n    cfg.validate()?;\n    Ok(cfg)\n}\n\n/// Load from a specific file
